title: Align Yazelix Next bar with main runtime styling
description:
Context:
- Current yzn substitutes @bar@ with yazelix-zellij-bar presetPath, which is the generic standalone preset.
- That preset renders format_left as {mode} {tabs}, so yzn shows the green NORMAL mode segment.
- Main Yazelix uses the integrated runtime bar shape: format_left is {tabs}, and the runtime/template path omits mode_normal entirely.
- Main also names the initial tab with the home icon marker from yazelix_zellij_config_pack, so the bar can display the home glyph through native {name}.

Desired contract:
- Yazelix Next top bar should visually match the main Yazelix runtime bar foundation as closely as practical without importing main repo complexity.
- The bar/preset owner should handle no-mode top bar formatting, tab label formats, colors, overflow, and widget-compatible placeholder shape.
- The yzn layout owner should name the first tab with the home icon marker because tab names are layout/session state, not generic bar state.

Likely implementation:
- Prefer consuming a bar-owned runtime/minimal preset from yazelix-zellij-bar instead of copying KDL into yazelix-next.
- If the child repo only exposes the full integrated renderer, add a tiny child-owned minimal runtime preset or renderer mode rather than expanding yzn.
- Update yzn layout to set the initial tab name to the same home icon marker used by main Yazelix.

Checks:
- Contract check should assert generated layout no longer contains {mode} or mode_normal in the top bar block.
- Contract check should assert the initial tab is explicitly named with the home icon marker.
- Manual dogfood: desktop yzn shows no NORMAL segment and first tab uses the home icon label.

Rejected for now:
- Do not hand-copy the full main Yazelix zellij_config_pack into yazelix-next.
- Do not add a config surface for this before the baseline visual contract is fixed.
notes:
Follow-up from manual screenshot: new tabs created through tab mode still displayed Tab #N. Added yzn tab-mode bind n to run NewTab { name ""; } and return to Normal, matching main Yazelix's home-marker tab naming without adding cwd/session management. Updated contract check, architecture, changelog, and LOC scorecard. Verified rustc checks for yzn-contracts/zellij-layout, cargo test for yzn-open, nix flake check with local yazelixZellijBar override, nix build .#yzn with override, inspected built config/layout, refreshed installed runtime with override, verified profile config/layout and yzn help.

Rendered widget tray slice: yzn now declares the fixed tray [editor, shell, term, codex_usage, cpu, ram] and calls the pinned yazelix-zellij-bar render-yazelix-runtime command at build time. The bar renderer owns the KDL command blocks and widget logic; yzn owns only the fixed labels, periods, runtime identity, and terminal session env. Verified this works against the pinned child commit with no local child override: rustc checks, cargo test, git diff --check, nix flake check, nix flake show --all-systems, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, installed profile upgrade, profile layout/config inspection, runtime_identity.json inspection, yzn launcher inspection, and yzn help.

Codex widget follow-up: manual dogfood showed the Codex segment missing while the other widgets rendered. Reproduced by running the widget with a stripped environment: without a status cache path it either failed to derive a cache or fell back to ~/.cache/yazelix_zellij_bar/codex_usage_cache_v2.json containing missing-tu backoff. Fixed yzn by bundling tokenusage 1.5.2 as `tu`, adding it to the yzn launcher PATH, and exporting a yzn-owned YAZELIX_STATUS_BAR_CACHE_PATH before Zellij starts. Verified the installed profile command prints Codex usage with only package-provided `tu` and a fresh yzn cache path.

Compact Codex display follow-up: manual dogfood showed token totals taking too much bar space. Changed yzn's Codex widget declaration from display `both` to `quota`, so it keeps the 5h/week reset-window percentages but removes token counts. Verified generated layout uses `--display quota --periods 5h,week`, direct widget output is compact, and the installed profile is refreshed.
close_reason: Implemented and verified rendered no-mode Yazelix Next bar with home-tab naming and fixed runtime widget tray.

title: Ship stacked Zellij layout
description: Add a repo-owned Zellij layout that starts Yazelix Next with a stacked tiled pane policy, and make yzn launch the Yazelix Zellij fork with that packaged layout.
acceptance_criteria:
layout.kdl is a repo-owned KDL file.
yzn launches the Yazelix Zellij fork with --layout pointing at the package-owned layout.kdl.
The layout uses Zellij native stacked pane semantics for tiled panes.
The installed package includes share/yazelix-next/layout.kdl.
nix flake show, Zellij layout/config checks, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, and profile refresh pass.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
notes: Runtime fix applied after dogfood failure: replaced --layout with --new-session-with-layout so yzn launched from inside an existing Zellij session starts a child session in Mars instead of adding a parent-session tab; changed NewPane "Stacked" to NewPane "stacked" to match this fork's accepted action argument. Verified with nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, live profile refresh, and wrapper/config inspection. Did not launch Zellij again from Codex after user reported parent-session tab side effects; awaiting user dogfood.
close_reason: Completed: user dogfooding confirms yzn opens well after switching to --new-session-with-layout and NewPane "stacked"; verification and profile refresh passed.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Reopened: Runtime fix: yzn did not open correctly from inside an existing Zellij session because --layout adds a tab to the parent session, and NewPane "Stacked" is rejected by this fork. Switch to --new-session-with-layout and lowercase NewPane "stacked".

title: Replace root appearance mode with Mars-native controls
description:
## Why

Root [appearance].mode created a second owner for Mars visuals and produced confusing behavior when a valid native Mars config already set force-theme and colors. Yazelix Next should stay slimmer: Mars appearance belongs to Mars config, while root config should not claim global light/dark coordination.

## Scope

- Revert the unreleased root appearance mode implementation.
- Remove runtime Mars light-config selection and bar appearance plumbing from root config.
- Expose selected Mars-native appearance fields in the Mars config tab: force-theme, core colors, and cursor color.
- Validate Mars theme choices and hex colors through the existing Mars native config writer.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard.
- Refresh installed yzn after runtime/package changes.

## Rejected Direction

Do not materialize a generated live Mars config from root semantic appearance state in Yazelix Next. That is main Yazelix-style ownership and is heavier than the current Next architecture.
close_reason: Completed: removed unreleased root appearance mode, reverted runtime light-mode Mars/bar plumbing, exposed Mars-native force-theme and core color fields through the Mars tab, validated Mars theme/color writes, updated docs/changelog/scorecard, and refreshed installed yzn.

title: Fix Ctrl Alt key-hint merging in the Zellij bar
description:
Context from investigation on June 25, 2026:

The reported screenshots were from yazelix-next, not main Yazelix. The bottom Zellij status-bar key hints visually merge with a shared modifier prefix when using simple Ctrl bindings, but with Ctrl Alt bindings the row expands into separate full chords such as `Ctrl Alt g > LOCK`, `Ctrl Alt p > PANE`, `Ctrl Alt t > TAB`, etc. The desired behavior is the compact merged shape, without renaming tabs or encoding tab decorations into tab names.

Relevant user-visible symptom:
- Ctrl-only mode hints render compactly as a shared prefix plus per-action keys.
- Ctrl Alt mode hints render each action as a separate full chord, wasting width and making the bar visually noisy.
- Separate related concern: activity dots can sometimes remain forever until the user focuses the tab, even after the pane that generated the activity has stopped. Track/verify this if the chosen bar implementation touches tab activity indicators.

Sources checked:
- Zellij modifier documentation: https://zellij.dev/documentation/changing-modifiers.html
- Zellij key-tooltips announcement/reference: https://zellij.dev/news/web-client-multiple-pane-actions/#key-tooltips
- zjstatus-hints plugin: https://github.com/b0o/zjstatus-hints

Findings:
- Zellij supports changing primary/secondary modifiers, including multi-modifier chords such as Ctrl Alt, when the terminal can report them. The official docs do not expose a setting for status-bar hint joining/formatting.
- This looks like a rendering issue in the built-in Zellij one-line status-bar hint renderer, not a keybind parsing issue.
- In the Zellij status-bar source, the one-line renderer computes common modifiers across all mode-indicator shortcuts, not only the row/mode currently being rendered. That means Ctrl-only can be extracted as a common prefix, while Ctrl Alt can fail to merge if another mode's exit/toggle key lacks Alt.
- Relevant upstream/fork source paths from the earlier inspection:
  - `default-plugins/status-bar/src/one_line_ui.rs`
  - `default-plugins/status-bar/src/first_line.rs`
  - `default-plugins/status-bar/src/main.rs`
- In `one_line_ui.rs`, the relevant flow was:
  - `render_mode_key_indicators(...)`
  - `common_modifiers_in_all_modes(&default_keys)`
  - `render_common_modifiers(...)`
  - fallback through full per-shortcut rendering when no common modifier survives
- Likely small fix: compute common modifiers only for the currently rendered mode's visible shortcuts, then render one shared prefix such as `Ctrl-Alt +` and stripped per-action keys. Add a regression test where Normal-mode indicators all use Ctrl Alt, while a non-current mode has only Ctrl or a different exit shortcut; the rendered Normal row should still use the shared Ctrl Alt prefix.

Alternative path:
- `zjstatus-hints` can feed Zellij keybinding hints into `zjstatus` through a named pipe and may avoid the built-in status-bar renderer entirely.
- That route is larger: it adds plugin/pipe/vendoring/permission/update-path complexity and may have limited formatting configurability. It is worth considering only if yazelix-next chooses a zjstatus-owned bar surface rather than a small Zellij fork fix.

Decision pressure:
- Do not solve this by renaming tabs.
- Prefer a clean ownership choice: either fix the Zellij fork's status-bar renderer, or intentionally replace the built-in bar with a zjstatus/zjstatus-hints style surface.
- For the smallest yazelix-next slice, first evaluate a focused Zellij fork patch plus a regression test before rebuilding the bar from first principles.

Acceptance criteria:
- Ctrl Alt default hints in yazelix-next render with a shared modifier prefix instead of repeating full chords per action.
- The fix does not require changing tab names.
- If the implementation affects tab activity markers, verify that stale activity dots clear without requiring focus on the tab.
- The chosen ownership boundary is recorded: Zellij fork renderer fix, zjstatus-hints integration, or first-party replacement.
notes: Runtime correction on June 25, 2026: the source fix at yazelix-zellij 5769da50 was not enough because Zellij embeds the status-bar plugin WASM from zellij-utils/assets/plugins/status-bar.wasm. Regenerated and pushed the embedded asset in yazelix-zellij 10c79f7e, then updated yazelix-next flake.lock to 10c79f7e and rebuilt .#yzn from the normal locked flake.
close_reason: Completed: fixed in yazelix-zellij commit 5769da50 by deriving one-line mode-hint common modifiers from the currently visible row instead of all modes. Added status-bar regression coverage for Ctrl-Alt hints with another mode using Ctrl. Pushed the fork, updated yazelix-next flake.lock to 5769da50, verified cargo test -p status-bar, nix build .#yzn with override, normal nix build .#yzn, contracts check, flake show --all-systems, and refreshed installed yzn.

title: Audit Tyce-reported Yazelix issues against Next
description:
## Why

Tyce has opened concrete issues against main Yazelix. Those reports are useful evidence because they describe real user friction rather than speculative roadmap work. Before Yazelix Next asks Tyce for serious macOS/Mars validation, audit those issues one by one and make sure Next is not recreating the same failure modes under a smaller architecture.

This bead is intentionally deferred. The user wants more feature progress first, so this should not displace the next runtime feature slices. Use it when the runtime has enough behavior that a Tyce issue comparison is meaningful, or when preparing the Tyce macOS validation pass.

## User-Visible Behavior

A future user should not hit avoidable problems in Yazelix Next that were already reported in main Yazelix. When an old issue is main-only or avoided by Next's simpler design, the reason should be recorded so future agents do not reintroduce the same complexity or bug class.

## Relationship To Existing Beads

Related but separate from `yazelix-next-mars-macos-tyce-validation-9j2`.

That bead validates the current Mars-backed runtime on macOS with Tyce. This bead prepares for that by auditing Tyce's prior main Yazelix reports as a mistake-prevention checklist.

## Scope

In scope:
1. Find the issues Tyce opened against main Yazelix.
2. Read each issue for the actual failure mode, not only the title.
3. Classify each issue as one of:
   - applies to Yazelix Next now
   - could apply after upcoming features
   - avoided by Yazelix Next architecture
   - main-only legacy/config/materialization problem
4. For every issue that applies now, either fix the smallest Next contract or create a focused implementation bead if the fix is larger than the audit pass.
5. For every issue that could apply later, record the guardrail or future check in the relevant bead.
6. For issues avoided by design, record the reason in this bead or the relevant architectural/planning bead.
7. Feed the result into the Tyce macOS validation bead before asking for tester time.

Out of scope:
1. Do not mechanically port main Yazelix fixes.
2. Do not create a compatibility layer just because main Yazelix had one.
3. Do not broaden Yazelix Next config, Home Manager, Yazi, popup, or session ownership unless a current Next contract requires it.
4. Do not make this audit block feature work before the runtime has enough surface area to compare.

## Suggested Process

1. Query the main Yazelix issue tracker for issues authored by Tyce.
2. Build a short table of issue id, failure mode, Next applicability, and action.
3. Check current Next docs/runtime/config owners against each applicable failure mode.
4. Prefer focused contract checks over broad regression suites.
5. Create or update beads only for confirmed Next risks.

## Verification

- A classified list of Tyce-authored main Yazelix issues exists in this bead's notes or linked bead updates.
- Each applicable failure mode has either a fix, a focused follow-up bead, or a rejected-by-design note.
- No broad main Yazelix compatibility surface is introduced by the audit itself.
- If runtime code changes are made in follow-up work, run the exact checks required by the changed surface.

## Stop Conditions

Stop if the issue list is too noisy to identify Tyce-authored reports reliably. Stop if a finding would require adding broad compatibility or ownership before the user explicitly chooses that direction. Stop if the runtime is still too feature-thin for the comparison to be meaningful; keep the bead deferred until more feature work lands.

title: Support narrow user Zellij plugin sidecar
description:
## Why

Power users can already extend managed Yazi with plugin directories plus init.lua, but managed Zellij currently rejects all user plugin declarations through the guarded zellij/config.kdl sidecar. A narrow plugin-only sidecar gives power users an escape hatch without making YZN own arbitrary Zellij keymaps, layouts, env, or a broad plugin manager.

## User-Visible Behavior

If ~/.config/yazelix-next/zellij/plugins.kdl exists, yzn accepts a native-shaped plugins/load_plugins fragment, validates that it only declares additional plugins, rejects collisions with Yazelix-owned plugin ids, and splices the entries into the generated managed Zellij config. If the file is absent, behavior is unchanged.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add ~/.config/yazelix-next/zellij/plugins.kdl as a plugin-only sidecar.
- Accept only top-level plugins { ... } and load_plugins { ... } blocks.
- Inject plugin entries into packaged plugins/load_plugins blocks.
- Reject collisions with owned plugin ids such as yzpp and yazelix_pane_orchestrator.
- Keep zellij/config.kdl guarded and still rejecting plugins/load_plugins.
- Do not add plugin keybindings in this slice.
- Do not add Ratconfig UI or TOML schema.
- Update README/CHANGELOG/ARCHITECTURE/LOC scorecard.

## Verification

- Focused contract check for accepted plugin sidecar injection and collision/unsupported-top-level rejection.
- nix flake check.
- nix flake show --all-systems.
- nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs.
- Temp profile install and installed runtime refresh if runtime package changes.

## Maintenance Notes

Rejected alternatives: semantic TOML [zellij.plugins] first, Ratconfig UI first, allowing arbitrary keybinds/env/layout in the plugin sidecar, or broad Zellij config merging. This is intentionally a power-user native KDL fragment first.
close_reason: Completed: added zellij/plugins.kdl plugin-only sidecar, Advanced file action, validation/injection, docs and scorecard. Verification: cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml; nix build .#checks.x86_64-linux.contracts --no-link --print-build-logs; nix flake check; nix flake show --all-systems; nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs; temp profile install; nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Scope adjustment during implementation: include a Ratconfig Advanced file action for zellij/plugins.kdl so the sidecar is discoverable and can be created from yzn config. Still no TOML schema, no plugin keybinding surface, and no broad Zellij merge.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Fresh-eyes correction after close: the first parser counted braces inside quoted strings and did not accept inline comments on block closes. Tightened the sidecar scanner so comments and quoted string values do not affect block depth, preserved injected plugin bodies, fixed owned-id/unmatched-brace diagnostics to use actual sidecar line numbers, and covered this in the contract fixture.

title: Support grouped modifier clusters in the Zellij bar
description: The one-line Zellij status bar currently supports at most one shared modifier prefix for the visible mode-hint row. If high-frequency/default Zellij keys such as Ctrl p, Ctrl t, and Ctrl q stay on plain Ctrl while the rest use Ctrl Alt, the renderer should show readable modifier clusters instead of degrading into one noisy mixed row. Keep the key-remap decision separate unless the selected slice explicitly pairs this with the Ctrl p/t/q remap.
design: Code context from yazelix-zellij/default-plugins/status-bar/src/one_line_ui.rs: render_mode_key_indicators currently has exactly two paths: one shared modifier prefix for the whole visible mode-hint row, or no shared prefix. common_modifiers_for_shortcuts now derives that one prefix from the current row for yazelix-next-1mb, which fixes all-Ctrl-Alt rows but cannot cleanly display mixed Ctrl and Ctrl-Alt rows. add_shortcut_with_inline_key and add_shortcut_with_key_only already render stripped keys after a shared prefix, so grouped rendering can reuse them. Better target shape: group rendered hints by modifier set, with a stable group order of Ctrl, Ctrl Alt, then Alt. Within each group, preserve the relative action order from the source row. For a Ctrl p/t/q keymap, the mode row should render as a Ctrl group for PANE/TAB/QUIT and a Ctrl Alt group for LOCK/RESIZE/SEARCH/SESSION. Alt is reserved for yzn-level hints such as Alt m and Alt Shift h if those are brought into the same surface.
acceptance_criteria: Mixed modifier rows render readable modifier groups: Ctrl, Ctrl Alt, and Alt where present; within each group, action order is stable; regression coverage includes Ctrl p/t/q plus Ctrl Alt for the remaining normal-mode hints; existing all-Ctrl-Alt single-prefix behavior from yazelix-next-1mb still passes; no tab-name hacks or zjstatus replacement.
notes: Recommendation: worth doing if we choose Ctrl p/t/q. The status bar should group by modifier set rather than preserve the original interleaved row literally. That keeps the visual model simple: Ctrl, Ctrl Alt, Alt.
close_reason: Completed: grouped modifier clusters and compact secondary hints shipped through child commit addea0e9 and parent commit f7a43ab. Manual dogfooding confirms the status bar is good.

title: Activate host mise in managed Nu
description:
## Contract

When `shell.program = "nu"`, Yazelix Next should activate host `mise` automatically if `mise` is available on the inherited `PATH`. `mise` remains host-owned: Yazelix Next does not package it in the runtime closure, does not configure it, and does not add a user-facing setting in this slice. Missing or failing `mise` is skipped so managed Nu startup remains robust.

## Scope Chosen

- Implement only the default managed Nu path.
- Insert `mise activate nu` output into the generated runtime `config.nu` after packaged Nu config and before optional user Nu config.
- Keep Bash, Zsh, and Fish unchanged as ordinary interactive packaged shells for now.
- Add a focused contract check with a fake host `mise` binary.
- Document the host-owned boundary.
- Rejected for this slice: bundled `mise`, semantic settings, and a cross-shell initializer framework.
notes: Fresh-eyes correction: implementation inserts host `mise activate nu` output after packaged Nu config and before optional user Nu config, preserving the packaged-first contract. The original close reason used "prepends" imprecisely.
close_reason: Completed: managed Nu now prepends host mise activate nu output when mise is on PATH, skips missing/failing mise, documents the host-owned boundary, and validates with a fake host mise contract check.

title: Make yzn menu a live-filter command palette
close_reason: Replaced the numbered interactive menu with a packaged fzf live-filter command palette while keeping the non-TTY numbered fallback for scripts and contract tests; updated help/docs/tutor wording and refreshed the installed runtime.

title: Guard Zellij sidecar runtime-owned nodes
description:
## Why

The Zellij sidecar guard currently blocks obvious integration owners such as keybinds and plugins, but it still allows `support_kitty_keyboard_protocol` and `env`. `support_kitty_keyboard_protocol` is explicitly YZN-owned because modified chords like `Alt Shift K` and `Alt Shift J` rely on it. `env` can override child-pane environment variables and collide with YZN-managed editor/open/status behavior. Before exposing editable Zellij sidecar fields in `yzn config`, the guard should reject these runtime-owned nodes.

## Evidence

- `runtime/yzn-zellij-config.rs:7`: `FORBIDDEN` includes `keybinds`, `default_shell`, `default_layout`, `layout`, `plugins`, `load_plugins`, `session_name`, and `attach_to_session`, but not `support_kitty_keyboard_protocol` or `env`
- `config.kdl:2`: packaged YZN Zellij config sets `support_kitty_keyboard_protocol true`
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:132`: contract C9 says Kitty keyboard protocol is explicitly enabled and validates `Alt Shift K`/`Alt Shift J` popup bindings
- `../yazelix-zellij/zellij-utils/src/kdl/mod.rs:4470`: upstream/fork Zellij parses `env { ... }` as a map of environment variables
- `flake.nix:271`: managed runtime exports `YZN_OPEN_LOG`; adjacent runtime setup also exports YZN/YAZELIX editor, state, status, and bridge environment

## Scope

In scope:
- Add `support_kitty_keyboard_protocol` and `env` to the forbidden Zellij sidecar node list
- Extend the existing sidecar contract check to reject both new forbidden nodes
- Update README and ARCHITECTURE guard lists/contracts so docs match runtime behavior
- Update CHANGELOG because this changes user-visible sidecar rejection behavior

Out of scope:
- Do not add a KDL parser or structured KDL merge engine
- Do not change the packaged `config.kdl` values
- Do not block safe scalar preference nodes such as `pane_frames`, `mouse_mode`, `scroll_buffer_size`, or `copy_on_select`
- Do not implement the editable config UI tabs in this bead

## Implementation Notes

1. Keep the guard token-based and cheap; the existing `first_token` behavior is sufficient for this slice
2. Update `checks/yzn-contracts.rs` where sidecar accepted/rejected cases are validated
3. Keep accepted sidecar fixtures focused on safe preference nodes
4. Use exact forbidden-node names in error assertions so future drift is visible

## Verification

- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and include the updated sidecar contract rejection
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0
- `git diff --check` should exit 0

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if Zellij changes make `support_kitty_keyboard_protocol` unrelated to modified-key behavior
- Stop if rejecting `env` would break an already supported YZN user workflow documented in this repo
- Stop if the implementation starts parsing nested KDL instead of preserving the current small guard

## Maintenance Notes

Dependency decision: add no crates. This is a static guard-list and contract-test update. Rejected alternative: allow `env` with a denylist for `YZN_*`, `YAZELIX_*`, `EDITOR`, `VISUAL`, and `ZELLIJ_*`; that is more policy and parser surface than needed before users actually need config-level env injection.
close_reason: Completed: Zellij sidecar guard now rejects support_kitty_keyboard_protocol and env in addition to existing integration-owned nodes. Updated contract coverage, README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard. Verification passed: git diff --check; nix flake check; nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs.

title: Add semantic custom popup config surface
description:
## Why

The `yzpp` child plugin can run arbitrary configured popups, but Yazelix Next only exposes its four packaged popup specs. Users currently cannot add their own small floating tools without taking over integration-critical Zellij config, which YZN intentionally blocks. The first YZN-owned custom popup surface should keep popup definitions semantic and narrow instead of allowing arbitrary `plugins` or `keybinds` sidecar edits.

## Evidence

- `config.toml`: the user-facing popup geometry settings are `[popup].side_margin` and `[popup].vertical_margin`.
- `config.kdl`: the packaged `yzpp` plugin config hardcodes managed popup specs under `popups {}` with generated popup geometry.
- `config.kdl`: packaged keybindings toggle `lazygit`, `config`, `agent`, and `menu` through fixed `MessagePlugin "yzpp"` payloads.
- `runtime/yzn-zellij-config.rs`: launch-time sidecar validation forbids `keybinds`, `plugins`, and `load_plugins`.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs`: Ratconfig-side Zellij parsing blocks the same top-level ownership areas.
- `README.md`: docs describe the sidecar as scalar Zellij preference editing, not plugin/keymap extension.
- `../yazelix-zellij-popup/README.md`: the child plugin already supports multiple configured popup specs and payload-based toggles.

## User-Visible Behavior

A user can define one or more custom YZN popup specs in `~/.config/yazelix-next/config.toml` and launch/toggle them through a YZN-owned surface without editing raw Zellij `plugins`, `load_plugins`, or `keybinds` blocks.

A likely minimal config shape to evaluate first:

```toml
[popups.my_tool]
command = "btm"
args = ["--basic"]
title = "btm_popup"
keep_alive = true
```

Exact naming can change during implementation, but the shape should remain semantic, argv-based, and intentionally smaller than raw `yzpp` KDL.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add a small semantic custom-popup config surface under root `config.toml`.
- Render valid custom popup specs into the packaged `yzpp` `popups {}` block at launch/materialization time.
- Reuse the existing managed popup geometry from `[popup].side_margin` and `[popup].vertical_margin` unless a per-popup geometry field is proven necessary.
- Support argv safely: command plus optional args, not shell strings.
- Support optional keep-alive/hide behavior if it maps directly to `toggle_close_behavior "hide"`.
- Define the smallest YZN-owned trigger surface, such as a front-door command that pipes to `yzpp`, menu entries, or explicit fixed slots. Choose one before coding.
- Add focused checks proving custom popup config renders into the packaged Zellij config and malformed custom specs fail clearly.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard if runtime behavior changes.
- Refresh the installed `yzn` runtime after flake/runtime changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not allow user sidecar ownership of top-level `plugins`, `load_plugins`, or `keybinds`.
- Do not add arbitrary Zellij keybinding management.
- Do not support custom plugin wasm locations or third-party Zellij plugin loading.
- Do not turn `yzn menu` into a generic popup manager unless the trigger decision proves that is the smallest viable surface.
- Do not duplicate `yzpp` parsing logic in YZN beyond the semantic config YZN chooses to own.
- Do not add shell-string command execution.

## Implementation Notes

1. Decide and document the irreducible trigger contract first. A popup definition without a practical launch/toggle path is not user-visible support.
2. Inspect the landed popup displacement fix before relying on `keep_alive = true` for persistent custom tools.
3. Keep `yzpp` as the owner of popup runtime behavior. YZN should only translate semantic config to plugin specs and provide the trigger surface.
4. Prefer extending existing runtime config materialization over adding a new helper crate unless the Rust code becomes clearer that way.
5. Validate ids and fields strictly enough that generated KDL is stable and readable.

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 if Ratconfig/config parsing is touched.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and include a focused contract check for custom popup rendering/validation.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` should exit 0 if package outputs change.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should exit 0.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` after runtime changes.
- Manual dogfood: define a harmless custom popup, launch/toggle it from the chosen YZN surface, verify it uses the configured global popup margins by default, and verify `keep_alive` preserves process state when switching to another popup.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if custom popups require weakening the Zellij sidecar denylist.
- Stop if the trigger surface cannot be made smaller than arbitrary keybinding management.
- Stop if the implementation starts becoming a generic `yzpp` config passthrough instead of a YZN semantic feature.
- Stop if command execution would require shell strings or unescaped KDL generation.

## Maintenance Notes

Rejected alternative: let users add raw `plugins`/`keybinds` to the Zellij sidecar. That conflicts with the current YZN ownership model and would make popup support depend on fragile user-authored integration KDL.
close_reason: Completed semantic custom popup config with required managed keybindings

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Scope decision: terminal-only yzn popup trigger is rejected as insufficient. First slice requires each semantic custom popup to declare a managed keybinding, rendered by YZN into packaged config.kdl while raw Zellij keybinds/plugins remain denied.

title: Add yzn config Ratconfig surface
description:
## Why

The public swap gate chooses both CLI config support and ratconfig UI, but only through one shared config core. Yazelix Next needs a small semantic config owner before it can provide a real config UI popup or user-editable Yazelix-owned settings without scattering config logic across CLI, UI, native files, and runtime helpers.

## User-Visible Behavior

A user and an agent can inspect and change supported Yazelix-owned settings through a stable command surface, and the same settings can later be edited through a ratconfig UI popup. Native config files remain directly editable for Mars, Starship, Nushell, guarded Zellij, and Yazi where those surfaces exist.

## Decision Context

Public swap gate: `yazelix-next-public-swap-gate-o4i`.

Recorded direction:
- CLI is the non-negotiable base because it is scriptable, testable, agent-friendly, useful for Home Manager/debugging, and easy to cover with contracts.
- Ratconfig is the human-facing layer because it makes config discoverable inside Yazelix, especially from a popup.
- Do not build separate CLI and UI logic.
- Preferred ownership is one schema, one validation path, one migration/contract path, and two frontends.
- Ratconfig is the config platform, not the config policy engine.

## Scope

In scope:
- Define the smallest semantic Yazelix Next config contract before coding.
- Add a shared Rust config core that owns schema, validation, defaults, reads, writes, and contract/migration use.
- Add minimal CLI commands for supported semantic settings, likely `get`, `set`, `unset`, `path`, and `open`, but keep the final command list small.
- Integrate ratconfig as the shared editor/patch/contract library only where it reduces code and does not take over Yazelix policy.
- Choose the initial storage file and format deliberately. Prefer a single Yazelix-owned semantic config file under `~/.config/yazelix-next/`.
- Keep native config files as native surfaces, not translated generic config blobs.
- Expose enough model/data for a future ratconfig UI popup to reuse the same core.
- Add focused contract checks for defaults, get/set/unset, validation failures, and contract/migration behavior if migrations exist.
- Update `README.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and the LOC scorecard.

Out of scope:
- Do not implement the full ratconfig TUI popup in this bead unless the user explicitly chooses to combine it.
- Do not build a broad settings surface. Start with a tiny set of real semantic settings.
- Do not manage Mars, Starship, Nushell, Zellij, or Yazi native config semantics through this core by default.
- Do not implement a generic format translator between native config files.
- Do not port main Yazelix's full `settings.jsonc` contract or Home Manager config machinery.
- Do not add popup launching here except for the command that a future popup will run.

## Config Surface Tiers

1. Semantic Yazelix config: edited by CLI and ratconfig UI, backed by ratconfig contracts/migrations. Examples include bar widgets, popup commands, sidebar width, and selected keybinding choices.
2. Native config surfaces: Mars TOML, Starship TOML, Nushell files, guarded Zellij KDL, and Yazi files. Users can edit these directly. Ratconfig may show status and open them, but should not blindly own their semantics.
3. Guarded integration config: Zellij and Yazi can break the runtime spine, so Yazelix Next must keep validators and ownership boundaries. Ratconfig can render safe semantic fields and diagnostics, but must not become a generic native merge engine.

## Implementation Notes

1. Start with one or two real settings only. Good candidates are settings already chosen as swap gates, such as popup table fields or bar widget selection, but avoid creating a config setting before the runtime owner exists.
2. Prefer TOML or JSONC based on ratconfig support and user ergonomics, not main Yazelix compatibility. If JSONC is chosen, justify why comments matter more than TOML simplicity.
3. The CLI should be deterministic and check-friendly. Avoid interactive prompts in the base commands.
4. Ratconfig can own comment-preserving patches, deterministic contracts, field model helpers, diagnostics, and later UI rendering.
5. Yazelix Next owns the schema, meaning of fields, runtime apply policy, safety guards, and native config boundaries.

## Verification

- CLI contract check covers reading defaults when no config exists.
- CLI contract check covers setting and unsetting a supported value.
- CLI contract check covers rejecting an unsupported path or invalid value clearly.
- If contract state/migration is introduced, a check covers joined state and one deterministic reconcile path.
- `nix flake check`.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` if the runtime package changes.
- Temp profile install if the package surface changes.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` after runtime changes.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the first slice requires a broad settings schema.
- Stop if ratconfig starts owning Yazelix runtime policy instead of generic config mechanics.
- Stop if native config handling becomes a generic merge/translation system.
- Stop if CLI and future UI need separate validation or write paths.
acceptance_criteria: yzn config opens the Ratconfig-backed config UI. config.toml auto-creates with Yazelix Next defaults and Ratconfig contract state when the config owner runs. The first real semantic config field, open.log_level, controls managed YZN_OPEN_LOG. Contracts, README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard are updated.
notes: Final naming decision: the owned semantic config file is config.toml, not settings.toml, because it is shorter and friendlier. Simplification passes removed the test-only hidden --check command, collapsed the one-field FieldSpec abstraction, inlined the one-call contract builder, narrowed package-internal --get to open.log_level, removed the unreachable native sidecar model after the advanced tab was removed, and collapsed duplicate field validation. Fresh-eyes pass made package-internal --get reject hand-edited invalid open.log_level values instead of exporting them to the runtime.
close_reason: Completed the user-chosen first config slice: yzn config opens the Ratconfig config UI, config.toml auto-creates with defaults and Ratconfig contract state, open.log_level drives managed YZN_OPEN_LOG, and checks/docs/package wiring are updated. Broader CLI commands, popup expansion, and native config ownership remain out of scope until chosen.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Reopened: Reopening only to correct the close reason after the user chose config.toml instead of settings.toml.

title: Consolidate yzn-open bridge test setup
description:
## Why

`yzn-open` has real bridge complexity, but its tests repeat temporary directory, fake Zellij, registry, socket, and log setup. Consolidating the setup can trim Rust physical LOC and make future bridge/session changes easier to check without changing runtime behavior.

## Evidence

- `crates/yzn-open/src/main.rs:164`: `try_bridge` has the core bridge selection/focus/send behavior and is dense enough that tests need to stay strong.
- `crates/yzn-open/src/main.rs:737`: the in-file test module starts after the production logic.
- `crates/yzn-open/src/main.rs:748`: test helpers build temp dirs, executable scripts, fake Zellij behavior, registries, and configs.
- `crates/yzn-open/src/main.rs:865`: bridge/logging tests begin after a large helper block and continue with repeated scenario setup.

## Scope

In scope:
- Refactor `crates/yzn-open/src/main.rs` tests to share scenario setup for bridge registry, fake Zellij command, socket/listener, and config construction.
- Preserve coverage for session isolation, stale registry handling, pane focus fallback, command payloads, logging, and editor fallback.
- Keep production behavior unchanged unless a simplification is obviously behavior-preserving and covered.
- Update README LOC scorecard if project file counts change.

Out of scope:
- Do not extract `yzn-open` to a child repo in this bead.
- Do not introduce a second bridge owner or compatibility shim.
- Do not weaken tests around session/pane isolation, because that is the risky user-facing behavior.
- Do not add heavy test dependencies.

## Implementation Notes

1. Create a small local test scenario/builder only if it deletes repeated setup and keeps each test readable.
2. Keep assertions near the behavior they prove; avoid hiding expected session ids, pane ids, or payloads behind opaque helpers.
3. Prefer merging helper plumbing over merging semantically distinct bridge tests.
4. Treat production refactors as optional and only do them when the tests make equivalence obvious.

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-open/Cargo.toml` should exit 0.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0 if workspace/contract checks include `yzn-open`.
- `wc -l crates/yzn-open/src/main.rs` should be lower without reducing bridge coverage.
- README LOC scorecard should match the repo's current counting method if project files changed.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the builder/helper makes a bridge case harder to understand than the repeated setup.
- Stop if the refactor changes test timing or Unix socket behavior in a flaky way.
- Stop if production simplification risks altering bridge fallback behavior.

## Maintenance Notes

Expected savings: roughly 80-150 physical Rust lines. This is a follow-up after the larger `yzn-config` and contract-harness cleanup beads, not the first lever.
close_reason: Completed: consolidated yzn-open bridge test setup, removed one-off bridge request/current-tab scaffolding, kept bridge assertions intact, and updated the LOC scorecard.

title: Enable Yazi zoxide history updates
description: Shift+Z in Yazi uses Yazi's built-in zoxide preset. Enable its update_db setup so directories visited inside the packaged Yazi sidebar also populate zoxide history.
close_reason: Fixed by enabling Yazi's built-in zoxide preset with update_db in the packaged init.lua. Verified flake show, yzn build, temp profile install, and refreshed installed runtime.

title: Adopt standalone Yazelix bar foundation
description:
## Why

Yazelix Next should eventually consume `yazelix-zellij-bar` because the bar is core to the Yazelix experience, but the first slice should be the standalone generic bar foundation rather than the full main-Yazelix status system. This keeps the Next architecture small while giving future widgets a stable visual and packaging base.

## Evidence

- `ARCHITECTURE.md:3`: Yazelix Next is currently a small Nix/Lix flake with one user-facing command, `yzn`.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:23`: `config.kdl`, `layout.kdl`, and `layout.swap.kdl` currently own Zellij behavior and layout.
- `layout.kdl:2`: the current top bar is native `plugin location="tab-bar"`.
- `layout.kdl:6`: the current bottom bar is native `plugin location="status-bar"` and should keep owning key hints for this slice.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix/docs/contracts/standalone_yazelix_zellij_bar_distribution.md:5`: `yazelix_zellij_bar` is the standalone package for the Yazelix-branded Zellij status-bar preset.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix/docs/contracts/standalone_yazelix_zellij_bar_distribution.md:30`: the standalone bar has no central config framework; KDL is the public surface.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix/docs/contracts/standalone_yazelix_zellij_bar_distribution.md:45`: the generic default must not require `yzx`, `yzx_control`, pane orchestrator, Yazelix caches, Nushell, tokenusage, or full Yazelix.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix/docs/contracts/status_bar_ownership.md:5`: full Yazelix status-bar ownership is split across zjstatus, the child bar package, runtime adapters, and the pane orchestrator, which is too much scope for the first Next slice.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add `github:luccahuguet/yazelix-zellij-bar` as an external flake input.
- Consume the child package as a packaged artifact, not copied Rust/KDL source.
- Replace the native top `tab-bar` pane in `layout.kdl` templates with the child standalone/generic `zjstatus` plugin block.
- Keep the native bottom `status-bar` for Zellij key hints.
- Add a focused contract check that the packaged layout uses the child bar plugin and still contains the native bottom status bar.
- Update `README.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and the LOC scorecard for the new runtime behavior.
- Refresh the installed `yzn` runtime after the flake changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not adopt `render-yazelix-runtime` yet.
- Do not add workspace, activity, sidebar/editor fact, provider usage, CPU, RAM, version, or custom command widgets in this slice.
- Do not add ratconfig, a bar settings file, a bar CLI surface, Home Manager options, or generated user config.
- Do not copy `yazelix-zellij-bar` Rust or preset files into this repo unless package consumption is impossible.
- Do not replace the bottom native Zellij `status-bar` in this slice.

## Implementation Notes

1. Add the `yazelix-zellij-bar` flake input and make it follow this repo's `nixpkgs` when practical.
2. Reference `yazelixZellijBar.packages.${system}.yazelix_zellij_bar` from `flake.nix`.
3. Materialize a layout-owned top bar plugin block from the child package output, preserving this repo's `@yazi@` substitutions and existing swap layout behavior.
4. Keep the bottom `plugin location="status-bar"` unchanged so the compact modifier-hint work remains visible.
5. Extend the existing layout/config contract check or add a small Rust check only if needed; prefer validating observable packaged layout properties over brittle string trivia.
6. Dogfood manually only after the build checks pass, because visual correctness is the real final signal for this slice.

## Verification

- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and include the bar/layout contract check.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0 and package the layout with the standalone Yazelix bar at the top and native status bar at the bottom.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should exit 0 for a clean profile install.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` should refresh the user's installed runtime after the package changes.
- Manual `yzn` dogfooding should show the Yazelix-branded top bar while preserving bottom key hints and the existing Yazi/sidebar layout behavior.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if `yazelix-zellij-bar` cannot be consumed as a package artifact without copying child source into this repo.
- Stop if the implementation requires adopting workspace/activity/provider widgets to get a usable top bar.
- Stop if replacing the top bar breaks sidebar swap layout behavior or bottom key-hint visibility.
- Stop if the child bar package contract has drifted materially from the cited standalone/generic boundary.

## Maintenance Notes

- This is a foundation bead. Add widgets later only when each widget has a clear owner, contract, and value.
- The preferred order after this slice is cheap standalone widgets first, provider widgets later, and Yazelix-specific workspace/activity facts last.
- The rejected direction is importing the full main-Yazelix integrated status system into Next before Next has a real status/event owner.
close_reason: Done: standalone Yazelix bar foundation is wired into yzn, desktop launches work with isolated preseeded plugin permissions, and the runtime was refreshed and dogfooded.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Implemented automated slice: yzn consumes github:luccahuguet/yazelix-zellij-bar as a flake input, substitutes the child packaged bar preset into the three top layout panes, keeps native bottom status-bar panes for key hints, and extends checks/zellij-layout.rs to catch regressions. Verified rustc checks, cargo fmt/test/clippy for yzn-open, nix build .#yzn, nix flake check, nix flake show --all-systems, temp profile install, runtime refresh, and installed layout inspection. Manual visual dogfooding is still the final acceptance before closing.

title: Extract yzn-config catalog into a typed module
description:
## Why

`crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs` is accumulating the Ratconfig catalog next to UI/event-loop/runtime file IO. The current file declares semantic config paths and defaults, bar widget values, tab/source ids, Advanced file action ids, root config fields, Mars fields, and Zellij fields before the actual implementation. That data should stay Rust-typed, but a narrow module would make ownership easier to scan as light mode, keys, bar widgets, and more config rows land.

## Evidence

- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs:32`: root config field path constants now include `open.log_level`, `shell.program`, and `bar.widgets`.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs:35`: bar widget defaults and allowed values live in the same file as UI and file IO.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs:67`: `CONFIG_FIELDS` defines the root Ratconfig field catalog inline.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs:94`: `MARS_FIELDS` defines editable Mars native fields inline.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs:107`: Zellij guarded/field policy is also inline.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs:577`: model construction consumes these tables directly.

## Scope

In scope:
- Create a small Rust module such as `crates/yzn-config/src/catalog.rs` or equivalent.
- Move typed catalog data only: path constants, tab/source/action ids, field specs, allowed values, starter text, and related helpers when they are purely catalog-shaped.
- Keep `FieldSpec`, `ConfigFieldSpec`, and `FileActionSpec` Rust-typed unless a tighter local structure is clearer.
- Preserve current behavior, diagnostics, defaults, labels, and tab/source ordering exactly.
- Keep the implementation in `main.rs` responsible for IO, UI loop, validation execution, parsing, and writes.

Out of scope:
- Do not move this catalog to TOML, JSON, YAML, or a generic data directory.
- Do not add a shared crate for this cleanup.
- Do not redesign Ratconfig tabs or add fields.
- Do not refactor parser/write logic unless tiny signature adjustments are needed.

## Implementation Notes

1. Move the catalog-shaped declarations into one Rust module with the smallest public surface.
2. Keep exported names explicit enough that readers can still see which owner uses each table.
3. Run rustfmt and keep the diff mostly mechanical.
4. If moving `FieldSpec`/`ConfigFieldSpec` makes visibility awkward, keep the struct definitions near the module that uses them and move only the constants/tables.

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0 if package wiring or generated helper behavior changes.
- `git diff --check` should exit 0.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the change starts altering config behavior, default values, or Ratconfig UI shape.
- Stop if the split requires broad visibility plumbing that makes the code harder to follow.
- Stop if a feature bead is actively rewriting the same field catalog; rebase this cleanup after that feature lands.

## Maintenance Notes

Rejected alternative: move config catalogs into data files. These tables are executable schema/policy, and Rust type checking is useful. The intended cleanup is file shape, not data externalization.
close_reason: Completed: moved the typed yzn-config catalog into crates/yzn-config/src/catalog.rs while preserving behavior, updated the LOC scorecard, and verified with cargo test plus git diff --check.

title: Load selected Yazi sidebar plugins
description: Load the selected Yazi plugins for the packaged sidebar: auto-layout, git, and starship. Exclude sidebar-state, sidebar-status, lazygit, and zoxide-editor because they depend on wider Yazelix orchestration or extra tool behavior.
close_reason: Implemented selected Yazi sidebar plugins using imported sources, not vendored plugin Lua: auto-layout.yazi from luccahuguet/auto-layout.yazi, starship.yazi from upstream, and Yazelix-patched git.yazi plus yazelix_starship.toml from a sparse fetch of yazelix-yazi-assets. Excluded sidebar-state, sidebar-status, lazygit, and zoxide-editor. Verified flake show, build, built config inspection, temp profile install, and profile refresh.

title: Apply Zellij config tab edits live
description:
## Why

The first editable Zellij config tab can safely be next-session only, but the long-term UX should apply supported Zellij preference edits without requiring a new YZN session. Today the launch path computes the active runtime config once by combining the packaged config with the user sidecar. If `yzn config` later edits `~/.config/yazelix-next/zellij/config.kdl`, the already-running session will keep using the previous generated runtime config unless YZN regenerates the active file and Zellij reload behavior is verified.

## Evidence

- `flake.nix:278`: launch computes `zellij_config` by running `yzn-zellij-config` over the packaged config, user sidecar, and `$YAZELIX_STATE_DIR/zellij/config.kdl`
- `runtime/yzn-zellij-config.rs:31`: the helper reads the sidecar only when invoked
- `runtime/yzn-zellij-config.rs:34`: the helper writes the generated runtime config path
- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs:60`: `yzn-config` owns the interactive UI loop but currently has no path to regenerate the active Zellij config after an edit
- `yazelix-next-o7q`: planned Zellij tab initially marks safe sidecar edits as next-session

## Scope

In scope:
- After the Zellij tab writes a supported sidecar field, regenerate the active runtime Zellij config for the current session when enough environment is available
- Export or otherwise pass the packaged config path, sidecar path, runtime config path, and helper path to `yzn-config` through the YZN runtime environment
- Verify whether the Yazelix Zellij fork watches the active config file and applies changed scalar options live; if not, document the exact supported fallback behavior
- Surface a clear Ratconfig apply status: live, regenerated but restart/session needed, or failed with actionable error
- Add focused checks around environment/path handling and helper invocation without launching a GUI session
- Update README/ARCHITECTURE/CHANGELOG if live apply becomes supported behavior

Out of scope:
- Do not call `yzx restart` or kill the user's live Zellij session
- Do not implement live apply for Mars in this bead
- Do not make guarded/integration-owned Zellij nodes editable
- Do not add a generic file-watcher or daemon to YZN
- Do not make Ratconfig own runtime refresh policy

## Implementation Notes

1. Start by proving the current Zellij fork's config reload behavior from source or a focused non-GUI check
2. Add explicit runtime environment variables only for the paths/helpers `yzn-config` needs; avoid shell string interpolation inside Rust
3. Reuse `runtime/yzn-zellij-config.rs` rather than duplicating merge/guard logic in `yzn-config`
4. Treat missing runtime paths as a next-session status, not as a hard config-edit failure
5. Keep failure messages bounded and visible in the config UI notice/diagnostic path

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 with helper/env routing coverage
- `nix flake check` should exit 0
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0
- Manual dogfooding in a fresh YZN session should confirm whether a safe scalar edit applies live or is clearly reported as requiring a new session

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the Zellij fork does not reload active config changes and no cheap non-destructive reload path exists
- Stop if implementing live apply requires broad Zellij fork changes rather than a YZN runtime refresh
- Stop if the feature requires launching or restarting GUI sessions from automated checks
- Stop if path passing would expose user-specific host state in packaged outputs

## Maintenance Notes

Dependency decision: add no crates by default. Use environment variables and the existing helper. This is intentionally P3 because next-session config edits are acceptable for the first source-backed tab slice.

title: Use Advanced tab to open native shell config files
description:
## Why

`yzn config` has an empty Advanced tab today. That tab should become a narrow escape-hatch surface for native user config files that Yazelix intentionally does not parse, starting with managed Nushell and Starship files.

## User-Visible Behavior

A user opens `yzn config`, switches to Advanced, and sees rows for host-owned native config files such as `nu/env.nu`, `nu/config.nu`, and `starship.toml`. Activating a row creates the file when appropriate and opens it in the configured editor path. Ratconfig shows the file status, but Yazelix Next owns the file list, default contents, and editor command.

## Scope

In scope:
- Consume the Ratconfig file-action row primitive from `ratconfig-den` once it is available in the locked Ratconfig input.
- Populate the existing Advanced tab instead of leaving it empty.
- Add file rows for:
  - `~/.config/yazelix-next/nu/env.nu`
  - `~/.config/yazelix-next/nu/config.nu`
  - `~/.config/yazelix-next/starship.toml`
- Respect `YAZELIX_NEXT_CONFIG_HOME` for all displayed and created paths.
- Let Yazelix Next create missing parent directories and files with minimal helpful starter comments or empty content where better.
- Open files through a clearly owned editor command, preferring the managed editor path when available and producing a clear error when no editor can be launched.
- Keep Nu and Starship parsing out of Ratconfig and out of this slice.
- Add focused checks for row presence, path resolution, missing-file creation behavior, and emitted/open handling without launching a GUI session.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard if user-visible config behavior changes.
- Refresh installed `yzn` after runtime flake changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not parse or validate Nushell or Starship config contents.
- Do not build a general file manager or command palette in `yzn config`.
- Do not expose arbitrary host files outside the Yazelix Next config root.
- Do not add editable semantic shell integration toggles in this bead.
- Do not make Ratconfig own Yazelix file IO, default contents, or editor policy.

## Implementation Notes

1. This bead is blocked until Ratconfig provides the host-handled file-action row primitive tracked by `ratconfig-den`.
2. Keep the Advanced tab absent or hidden until it has at least one real row; do not ship an empty tab.
3. Prefer creating files only after explicit row activation, not during normal model construction.
4. Treat read-only or failed editor launch as a visible Ratconfig notice/error, not as a panic.
5. Keep default starter contents tiny and format-native.

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml` covers Advanced file rows and host-owned creation/open intent handling.
- `nix flake check` passes after Ratconfig is locked.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` passes if runtime packaging changes.
- Temp profile install passes if package surface changes.
- Manual dogfooding, when requested, confirms the Advanced tab opens `nu/env.nu`, `nu/config.nu`, and `starship.toml` from inside `yzn config`.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if Ratconfig's primitive starts owning file IO or editor launching instead of emitting host intents.
- Stop if the implementation requires parsing Nushell or Starship content.
- Stop if editor launching cannot be made explicit and testable without GUI/session side effects.
close_reason: Implemented Advanced tab file actions for nu/env.nu, nu/config.nu, and starship.toml. yzn-config creates starter files only on explicit activation, validates file-action ownership, opens through the managed yzn-config-ui editor wrapper, and keeps libexec yzn-config as the --get helper. Verified cargo test, normal nix flake check/show/build, and temp profile install after locking ratconfig 04119b6.

title: Restore Zellij bars in stacked layout
description: The custom stacked layout dropped Zellij's default tab-bar and status-bar plugin panes. Restore them while keeping the stacked main pane.
acceptance_criteria:
layout.kdl includes a top tab-bar plugin pane.
layout.kdl includes a bottom status-bar plugin pane.
The main pane remains stacked.
The installed package includes the restored layout.
nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, and profile refresh pass.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
close_reason: Completed: user dogfooding confirms the restored Zellij bars look good; layout restores tab-bar and status-bar plugin panes while keeping the stacked main pane; package build, temp profile install, and live profile refresh passed.

title: Simplify yzn-contracts harness repetition
description:
## Why

`checks/yzn-contracts.rs` is the largest Rust file and now owns many unrelated contract surfaces in one harness. The checks are valuable, but repeated command/assertion shapes and large inline case tables create physical LOC pressure and make future agents pay a high reading cost before changing one surface.

## Evidence

- `checks/yzn-contracts.rs:129`: `expect_front_door` starts a broad front-door/menu/help contract with several repeated allow/forbid assertions.
- `checks/yzn-contracts.rs:904`: `expect_startup_diagnostics` owns a large inline failure-case matrix for config, keybinding, popup, and welcome diagnostics.
- `checks/yzn-contracts.rs:1735`: `expect_agent_bootstrap` combines multiple provider/fallback/persisted-provider cases in a long helper near the bottom of the monolithic harness.
- The file is about 1,967 physical lines, making it the largest Rust owner in the repo.

## Scope

In scope:
- Simplify repeated assertion and command-running patterns inside `checks/yzn-contracts.rs`.
- Prefer small local helpers or typed case structs where they delete repeated setup and improve scanability.
- Keep the contract surfaces and expected diagnostics intact.
- Split the file only if the split creates clearer ownership without adding adapter glue or weakening the one-command check.
- Update README LOC scorecard if project file counts change.

Out of scope:
- Do not remove user-visible contract coverage to chase the line target.
- Do not add generated fixtures, external snapshot files, or a broad test framework.
- Do not change packaged runtime behavior.
- Do not rewrite the harness around a new abstraction unless it deletes real repetition.

## Implementation Notes

1. Start by compacting repeated allow/forbid string assertions and diagnostic case setup.
2. Consider a tiny `FailureCase`/`ContractCase` helper only if it makes the body read as data plus contract.
3. Keep failure labels precise so a broken runtime still points at the failed surface.
4. If splitting, group by existing runtime surface: front door/menu, startup diagnostics/config, sidecar/layout/tool contracts, agent/bootstrap.
5. Run the exact contract check before and after simplification.

## Verification

- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and run the contract harness successfully.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0 if packaging surfaces are touched indirectly.
- `wc -l checks/yzn-contracts.rs` or the split check files should show a real net reduction.
- README LOC scorecard should match the repo's current counting method if project files changed.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if a proposed helper obscures which user-visible command or diagnostic is being checked.
- Stop if a split requires duplicating temp-dir, command, or fixture setup across files.
- Stop if the reduced harness no longer fails with actionable labels for each contract surface.

## Maintenance Notes

Expected safe savings: roughly 150-300 physical Rust lines. This is the second-best lever after `yzn-config` tests; the goal is simpler contract ownership, not thinner coverage.
close_reason: Completed: simplified yzn-contracts repeated assertion, runtime-case, executable fixture, and startup diagnostic harness setup while preserving contract coverage; Rust scorecard drops from 10274 to 10200.

title: Expose Mars cursor controls in config popup
description:
## Why

The config popup should let users adjust the useful Mars cursor settings without reintroducing a separate yzn cursor owner. These settings are Mars-native and should stay in the Mars tab for now.

## Scope

In scope:
- Expose yazelix.cursor.divider with the Mars-supported values vertical and horizontal.
- Expose yazelix.cursor.colors as the two split-cursor colors accepted by Mars.
- Validate the color array as exactly two opaque #RRGGBB strings.
- Keep effects.trail-cursor and yazelix.cursor.cursor_color in the Mars tab.
- Update docs, changelog, and LOC scorecard for the user-visible config surface.

Out of scope:
- Do not add a separate cursor tab yet.
- Do not expose yazelix.cursor.family or yazelix.cursor.transition while Mars only accepts one real value for each.
- Do not rebuild the main yzx cursor registry in yzn.

## Decision

Rejected for this slice: a standalone cursor tab. Cursor fields stay under Mars until a cross-terminal cursor registry or child-owned cursor surface exists.
close_reason: Completed: Mars tab now exposes cursor divider and two-color split cursor array with focused validation and docs.

title: Add AppImage Linux distribution
description:
## Why

The user wants an AppImage packaging track as a Linux non-Nix option. AppImage can provide a single-file Linux desktop artifact that users can download and run without a distro package manager. For Yazelix, this should be treated as a wrapper around the relocatable runtime bundle, not as a new source-of-truth package system.

## User-Visible Behavior

A Linux user can download a Yazelix AppImage, mark it executable, and run it:

```sh
chmod +x Yazelix-x86_64.AppImage
./Yazelix-x86_64.AppImage
```

The AppImage launches the Mars-backed Yazelix app path when possible and exposes a way to run the `yzx` CLI from the same artifact or installed desktop integration. It should not require Nix on the user's machine.

## Source Context

- Existing prerequisite bead: `yazelix-next-nix-free-installer-nx4` designs the Nix-built relocatable release bundle and Nix-free install path.
- Official AppImage docs describe the format as a Linux application packaged into one file that can be downloaded, made executable, and run without installation.
- AppImage packaging generally uses an AppDir layout plus `appimagetool`; Yazelix should reuse the relocatable runtime layout rather than inventing a separate filesystem contract.

## Scope

In scope:
- Build a Linux AppImage from the relocatable Yazelix runtime bundle.
- Start with the most useful Linux architecture, likely `x86_64-linux`, then add `aarch64-linux` only if the runtime and toolchain support it cleanly.
- Include Mars app launch, the final public `yzx` CLI, desktop entry metadata, icon, packaged runtime tools/assets, Zellij plugins/WASMs, and config/layout assets.
- Define how CLI invocation works from an AppImage artifact, such as `--appimage-extract-and-run`, a wrapper symlink installed by optional integration, or a documented `Yazelix.AppImage -- <yzx args>` style if feasible.
- Verify that `yzx help`, `yzx enter`, and `yzx launch` or their AppImage equivalents run without Nix store paths.
- Document install/run/update/uninstall expectations honestly: download-and-run first, desktop integration later if needed.
- Keep AppImage as a Linux package option, not the canonical release system.

Out of scope:
- Do not make AppImage the source of truth for runtime composition.
- Do not add Flatpak or Snap in this bead.
- Do not solve macOS packaging here.
- Do not package Ghostty or broaden terminal support for AppImage.
- Do not add a full auto-updater in the first slice.
- Do not assume sandbox isolation; AppImage should run as the user and use normal user config paths.

## Implementation Notes

1. Wait until the relocatable runtime bundle contract exists and proves the runtime does not depend on embedded `/nix/store` paths.
2. Use Nix as the build factory for the AppDir/AppImage if that keeps dependencies deterministic.
3. Map the relocatable Yazelix bundle into an AppDir with a clear `AppRun`, `.desktop`, icon, and internal runtime root.
4. Decide whether AppRun defaults to `yzx launch` and how to pass through CLI args to `yzx` without surprising users.
5. Test both direct execution and extracted execution, because AppImage/FUSE behavior can differ across distributions.
6. Treat desktop integration as optional unless the first AppImage usability test proves it is necessary.

## Verification

- The AppImage build command should produce a Linux AppImage artifact without manual steps.
- Running the AppImage on a Linux test machine should start the Mars-backed app path or print a clear diagnostic if GUI prerequisites are missing.
- Running the CLI passthrough path should make `yzx help` exit 0 without Nix installed.
- `yzx doctor` or `yzx status`, when implemented, should pass from the AppImage runtime.
- The runtime should not reference `/nix/store` paths after extraction except in build metadata that is not used at runtime.
- AppImage desktop metadata should show the expected name, icon, and categories.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the runtime is not relocatable without embedded Nix store paths.
- Stop if AppImage requires broad invasive changes to Mars, Zellij, or the runtime launcher before the core release path is stable.
- Stop if FUSE/AppImage runtime behavior makes CLI use unreliable and no simple extracted-run fallback works.
- Stop if building the AppImage starts duplicating flake package composition or release artifact layout.
- Stop if this starts becoming Flatpak/Snap/sandbox packaging work.

## Maintenance Notes

Rejected alternative: Flatpak first. Yazelix wants host filesystem, host shells, agent/editor workflows, and terminal integration; Flatpak sandbox policy would become the main task. Rejected alternative: Snap first. Strict confinement and classic-confinement review do not fit the first Linux package path as well as AppImage.

title: Add configurable bar widgets to config.toml
description:
## Why

Yazelix Next currently hardcodes the top-bar widget tray at package build time. The child `yazelix-zellij-bar` package already supports more widgets, including `claude_usage`, but Yazelix Next users cannot enable or reorder them from the semantic config. The user explicitly chose adding this to `config.toml` so the bar can be configured without editing generated Zellij KDL.

## User-Visible Behavior

A user can edit the Yazelix semantic config and choose the top-bar widget tray:

```toml
[bar]
widgets = ["editor", "shell", "term", "codex_usage", "cpu", "ram"]
```

The default preserves the current bar. Adding supported widgets such as `claude_usage` applies to newly launched Yazelix sessions without requiring users to edit `layout.kdl`, generated bar KDL, or the Zellij sidecar:

```toml
[bar]
widgets = ["editor", "shell", "term", "codex_usage", "claude_usage", "cpu", "ram"]
```

Invalid widget ids should fail through the same config validation path used by other semantic config fields, with a clear message naming the allowed ids.

## Evidence

- `flake.nix:266`: `yznBarRenderRequest` is rendered at package build time.
- `flake.nix:268`: `widget_tray` is currently hardcoded to `["editor" "shell" "term" "codex_usage" "cpu" "ram"]`.
- `flake.nix:281`: Claude usage settings are already passed to the render request, but they are inert unless `claude_usage` is present in `widget_tray`.
- `config.toml:4`: the semantic config currently has only `[open]`.
- `config.toml:8`: the semantic config currently has only `[shell]` after `[open]`; there is no `[bar]` section.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs:32`: semantic config fields are currently enumerated as constants such as `open.log_level` and `shell.program`.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs:53`: `CONFIG_FIELDS` currently supports the root semantic fields exposed through Ratconfig.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs:510`: `read_config_field` returns scalar field values through the hidden `--get` path used by runtime wrappers.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:54`: `yazelix-zellij-bar` owns rendered top bar KDL and widget command logic.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:55`: Yazelix Next currently declares a fixed tray rather than a user-configurable tray.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add a semantic root config field for the bar widget tray, probably `[bar].widgets`.
- Preserve the current hardcoded tray as the default.
- Validate widget ids against a small explicit allowlist that matches widgets supported by the pinned `yazelix-zellij-bar` runtime renderer.
- Include `claude_usage` in the allowed set so users can enable the Claude widget.
- Expose the field in `yzn config`/Ratconfig if Ratconfig can represent a string-list or equivalent multi-choice field cleanly.
- If Ratconfig cannot yet edit arrays well, still validate and consume `config.toml`, and show the setting as read-only or create a follow-up Ratconfig field-type bead.
- Make `yzn launch` and `yzn enter` consume the selected widget tray for newly launched sessions.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and the LOC scorecard.
- Add focused checks for default config creation, valid custom widget lists, invalid widget rejection, and generated runtime bar/layout content.
- Refresh the installed `yzn` after runtime flake changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not make Ratconfig edit raw Zellij bar KDL.
- Do not create a full bar theme/layout editor.
- Do not add arbitrary custom widget commands in this bead.
- Do not make workspace or OpenCode Go usage part of the default tray unless separately chosen.
- Do not duplicate widget rendering logic from `yazelix-zellij-bar` in Yazelix Next.
- Do not add Home Manager declarative bar options in this bead.

## Implementation Notes

1. Start by defining the semantic config contract and default in `config.toml` and `crates/yzn-config`.
2. Be careful with ownership: `yazelix-zellij-bar` should keep owning valid rendering behavior and widget command internals; Yazelix Next should only own the selected widget list and runtime wiring.
3. The current bar KDL is rendered by Nix from `yznBarRenderRequest`, so a runtime `config.toml` field cannot affect it by itself. The implementation likely needs a small runtime materialization step that reads `bar.widgets`, renders or selects the bar KDL/layout for that launch, and points Zellij at the generated layout path.
4. Prefer reusing the existing runtime state dir and `runtime/yzn.rs` startup boundary for any generated active layout/bar file.
5. If invoking the bar widget renderer at runtime is too expensive or awkward, investigate a cheaper checked approach, but do not ship a config field that only affects future rebuilds rather than user launches.
6. Keep the first UI simple: a comma-separated validated string is acceptable only if Ratconfig lacks a proper string-list/multi-select field and the config file itself remains a TOML array.

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 and cover `[bar].widgets` defaults, valid list parsing, invalid widget rejection, and write/reset behavior if editable in Ratconfig.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and prove the packaged default still renders the current widget tray.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` should exit 0 if package outputs change.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should exit 0.
- `git diff --check` should exit 0.
- If runtime layout materialization changes, `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` should be run after the package is updated.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if this turns into a general Zellij layout editor or arbitrary widget-command system.
- Stop if supporting `[bar].widgets` requires copying substantial rendering logic out of `yazelix-zellij-bar`.
- Stop if Ratconfig cannot represent the field cleanly and the implementation would require a weak UI hack; create a focused Ratconfig field-type bead instead and keep config-file validation separate.
- Stop if the selected widgets can only affect Nix rebuilds and not normal user launches from `config.toml`.
- Stop if the pinned bar package does not expose a stable enough renderer/CLI contract for runtime materialization.

## Maintenance Notes

Rejected alternative: add `claude_usage` directly to the hardcoded default tray. That is a useful one-line experiment, but it does not solve user choice or future widget selection. Rejected alternative: put widget config in the Zellij sidecar; the widget tray is Yazelix semantic runtime policy, not raw Zellij config.
close_reason: Implemented configurable [bar].widgets with validation, runtime layout materialization for custom trays, docs, scorecard, and verification.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Implemented as a semantic config-file setting rather than a Ratconfig list editor: yzn-config validates [bar].widgets and emits JSON for runtime, default tray uses the packaged pre-rendered layout, and custom trays materialize a runtime layout via the pinned yazelix-zellij-bar renderer so widget KDL/rendering ownership stays upstream.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Fresh-eyes follow-up: custom trays also need the managed new-tab keybind to use the active runtime layout. Runtime now patches the generated Zellij config from the packaged layout path to the materialized runtime layout when [bar].widgets is custom; default trays still use packaged layout/config.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Simplification follow-up: removed the hand-written runtime JSON string parser for bar renderer output. Runtime now calls a tiny yzn-bar-render helper that reuses the same jq -er .plugin_block extraction already used for the packaged default bar, keeping rendering ownership with yazelix-zellij-bar while reducing runtime parser code.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Ratconfig follow-up complete: after Ratconfig added generic ordered string-list helpers, yzn-config now exposes bar.widgets in the Ratconfig UI with ConfigUiEditBehavior::OrderedStringList, validates/extracts via ratconfig::string_list_values_from_json, and keeps Yazelix ownership limited to widget ids/defaults and runtime materialization.

title: Add Homebrew tap and bottle distribution
description:
## Why

A Homebrew install is the cleanest non-Nix package-manager experience for developer users, especially on macOS. The user wants a Homebrew bottle path in addition to the existing Nix-free installer/release-bundle plan. This should wrap the same relocatable Yazelix runtime rather than creating a second runtime packaging implementation.

## User-Visible Behavior

A user can install the public Yazelix package through a tap:

```sh
brew install luccahuguet/yazelix/yzx
```

The installed `yzx` command runs without requiring Nix on the user's machine. `yzx help`, `yzx enter`, and `yzx launch` work from the Homebrew install. Upgrades and uninstall use normal Homebrew workflows.

## Source Context

- Existing prerequisite bead: `yazelix-next-nix-free-installer-nx4` designs the Nix-built relocatable release bundle and Nix-free install path.
- Official Homebrew bottle docs describe bottles as binary packages produced with `brew install --build-bottle <formula>` and `brew bottle <formula>`.
