title: Extend semantic popup role keybindings beyond agent
description:
## Why

`keybindings.agent` establishes the semantic role-remap model: users configure a YZN-owned role/action, and Yazelix Next renders the managed Zellij binding without exposing raw `keybinds`. The same model may be useful for other packaged popup roles once the agent-only slice proves the validation and rendering path.

## User-Visible Behavior

A user can remap selected YZN-owned popup roles such as `menu`, `config`, and `lazygit` through semantic keybinding fields while defaults remain unchanged. Each configured chord still toggles the same managed popup payload, and conflicts fail before launching a broken session.

## Scope

In scope:
- Evaluate extending the role-keybinding schema beyond `agent` to other packaged popup roles.
- Keep each role semantic: remap a YZN-owned popup action, not arbitrary programs or raw Zellij keybinds.
- Reuse the c30 validation/render path if it is still the right owner.
- Keep defaults for `menu`, `config`, and `lazygit` unchanged unless explicitly configured.
- Add focused checks for default bindings, one override, invalid syntax, and conflicts across editable roles.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard if user-visible behavior changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not expose raw Zellij `keybinds`, `plugins`, or arbitrary command launchers.
- Do not add custom popup definition support; that belongs to `yazelix-next-6aq`.
- Do not make all native Zellij/Yazi/Helix keys editable.

## Sequencing Notes

Do this after `yazelix-next-configurable-agent-popup-key-c30` lands and after dogfooding confirms the role-keybinding model feels right for one action first.
close_reason: Completed popup role keybinding configurability for config, agent, lazygit, and menu.

title: Add Ratconfig Keys tab with read-only native bindings
description:
## Why

`yzn config` should help users discover the Yazelix Next key model without forcing them to read README tables or remember which tool owns which binding. A Ratconfig Keys tab can be the daily "what was that key?" surface and, later, the narrow editor for safe Yazelix-owned semantic key choices.

## User-Visible Behavior

A user opens `yzn config` and sees a Keys tab listing important bindings grouped by workflow. Each row shows the action, key chord, owner, source, and whether it is editable. Native-tool bindings are visible but read-only. Yazelix-owned semantic bindings can become editable only when the runtime has one shared validation/render path for those actions.

Example rows:

- Toggle sidebar: `Alt Shift h`, owner `Yazelix`, editable when backed by semantic config.
- Toggle LazyGit popup: `Alt Shift j`, owner `Yazelix`, editable when backed by semantic config.
- Open config popup: `Alt Shift c`, owner `Yazelix`, editable once the popup exists.
- Pane mode: `Ctrl p`, owner `Zellij`, read-only.
- Tab mode: `Ctrl t`, owner `Zellij`, read-only.
- Yazi zoxide jump: `Alt z`, owner `Yazi`, read-only.
- Helix normal-mode behavior: owner `Helix`, read-only if shown.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add a Keys tab/model to the Ratconfig-backed `yzn config` UI.
- Represent key rows with at least action label, key chord, owner, source, group, editable flag, and read-only reason.
- Group rows by practical workflow, such as Workspace, Panes, Tabs, Popups, Sidebar, File manager, and Editor.
- Start from current packaged Yazelix Next bindings, not main Yazelix compatibility.
- Make read-only native bindings useful for discovery without letting Ratconfig rewrite Zellij, Yazi, Helix, Nushell, or Mars config directly.
- If editable keys are included in the first slice, limit them to semantic Yazelix-owned actions with one shared config-core validation path.
- Add focused checks that the Keys tab/model includes the expected important bindings and correctly marks native owners read-only.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard if files or user-visible config behavior change.

Out of scope:
- Do not build a general keybinding editor.
- Do not parse and rewrite arbitrary Zellij/Yazi/Helix native keymaps.
- Do not add a keymap compiler unless a later user-chosen semantic remap contract requires it.
- Do not make ratconfig own native tool config policy.
- Do not add legacy Yazelix key compatibility.
- Do not make the tutor depend on this tab, though the two should eventually agree on key text.

## Implementation Notes

1. Treat the Keys tab first as a discovery surface. Editable semantic remaps are a second step unless they are tiny and clearly owned.
2. Keep owner boundaries explicit: `Yazelix` rows may write semantic settings; `Zellij`, `Yazi`, `Helix`, `Nushell`, and `Mars` rows are display-only.
3. Prefer one checked table of known actions over scraping native config files if that keeps ownership clearer.
4. If editable semantic bindings are added, validate duplicate/conflicting chords before writing settings.
5. Coordinate with popup key choices from the first-party popup bead so config, agent, menu, LazyGit, and sidebar keys appear consistently.

## Verification

- Ratconfig/model check proves required key rows exist with expected owner/editable status.
- Check proves native-tool rows are read-only and include a reason.
- If editable semantic keys are implemented, checks cover valid set, invalid chord, duplicate/conflicting chord, unset/default behavior, and rendered runtime effect.
- `nix flake check` when packaged config/runtime changes.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` if the runtime package changes.
- Refresh installed `yzn` after runtime flake changes.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the slice becomes a generic keymap editor.
- Stop if implementing edits requires broad native config parsing or merge behavior.
- Stop if popup/action keys are still too unstable to expose as editable semantic settings; ship read-only discovery first or defer edits.
close_reason: Completed: yzn config now exposes a read-only Keys tab for current packaged bindings; Ratconfig renders disabled read-only rows as read-only; docs, changelog, LOC scorecard, tests, contract build, and runtime profile refresh completed.

title: Add lightweight CHANGELOG.md
description:
Add a small CHANGELOG.md so user-visible runtime changes are recorded outside chat history.

Acceptance criteria:
- Create a lightweight CHANGELOG.md format that fits the repo's minimal architecture, likely an Unreleased section plus dated release sections only when needed.
- Capture current major user-visible slices at a high level without turning it into release bureaucracy.
- Add a short maintainer rule only if needed: update the changelog when behavior, commands, keymaps, packaged tools, or runtime contracts change.
- Update the README LOC scorecard if project files change.

Rejected direction: do not add release automation, generated changelogs, or versioning machinery until the user chooses that scope.
close_reason: Completed: added lightweight CHANGELOG.md, documented the maintainer update rule, refreshed the README LOC scorecard, and verified the docs diff and LOC totals.

title: Make the agent popup key configurable
description:
## Why

`Alt Shift L` is currently the fixed Yazelix-owned chord for the persistent agent popup. That is fine as a default, but once the agent pane becomes more configurable, the key should eventually be configurable too. The important boundary is that users are remapping a semantic YZN action, not turning the packaged Zellij keymap into an arbitrary launcher surface.

## User-Visible Behavior

A user can keep the default `Alt Shift L` binding for the agent popup, or choose a different valid key chord in YZN config/Ratconfig. The binding still toggles the same managed `agent` popup action, and invalid or conflicting chords fail with a clear config error before launching a broken session.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add the smallest semantic config surface for remapping the YZN-owned agent popup action.
- Keep `Alt Shift L` as the default when no override is set.
- Render the configured chord into the managed Zellij config without opening raw `keybinds` sidecar ownership.
- Surface the row in Ratconfig's Keys tab as editable only when the validation/render path is shared and tested.
- Validate chord syntax and reject conflicts with other YZN-owned packaged keys.
- Keep the action payload/identity as `agent`; only the trigger chord changes.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and the LOC scorecard if user-visible config/runtime behavior changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not implement the first-run agent provider bootstrap or fallback chain here.
- Do not let users bind arbitrary programs to `Alt Shift L` or this action.
- Do not make all Zellij/Yazi/Helix/native-tool keys editable.
- Do not weaken the Zellij sidecar denylist for `keybinds`, `plugins`, or `load_plugins`.
- Do not rename the action from agent popup unless the agent provider config work has already changed the user-facing vocabulary.

## Implementation Notes

1. Prefer completing or folding in `yazelix-next-otx` if this work needs a shared command/key reference owner.
2. Treat `config.kdl` as the generated/runtime keymap owner and YZN semantic config as the user-editable source.
3. The likely first shape is a narrow key override such as a `keys` table entry for `agent_popup`, but choose the final name after checking the existing config schema.
4. If Ratconfig editing is not cheap, land the config/render/check slice first and leave the Keys tab row read-only with a clear follow-up.
5. Keep checks focused on the default binding, one custom binding, invalid syntax, and a duplicate/conflicting binding.

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 if config parsing or Ratconfig changes.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and include a contract check for the default and overridden agent key.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0 if packaged runtime config changes.
- `git diff --check` should exit 0.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` after runtime package changes.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the change starts becoming a generic keymap editor.
- Stop if conflict detection cannot be kept small and deterministic.
- Stop if the implementation requires raw Zellij sidecar keybind ownership.
- Stop if agent provider/config naming is still unsettled and would make the config key misleading.

## Maintenance Notes

Preferred direction: semantic remapping of a YZN-owned action through config/Ratconfig. Rejected direction: arbitrary command binding or raw Zellij keymap editing.
close_reason: Completed semantic keybindings.agent config, validation, runtime rendering, docs, and checks.

title: Consume yzpp default popup margins
description:
## Why

Yazelix Next owns the semantic `[popup].side_margin` and `[popup].vertical_margin` settings, but today it implements that by repeating or patching `side_margin`/`vertical_margin` inside every managed `yzpp` popup spec. If `yazelix-zellij-popup-default-popup-margins-id3` lands, Next can express the same product setting as a plugin-level `yzpp` default and keep per-popup KDL leaner.

## Evidence

- `config.kdl:12`: packaged config repeats `side_margin 1` in the config popup spec.
- `config.kdl:13`: packaged config repeats `vertical_margin 0` in the config popup spec.
- `runtime/yzn.rs:715`: runtime margin customization checks whether either semantic popup margin differs from the packaged defaults.
- `runtime/yzn.rs:718`: runtime patching searches for `side_margin <default>` markers across the config text.
- `runtime/yzn.rs:727`: runtime patching replaces all matching margin markers in the active Zellij config.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs:456`: custom popup rendering reads the same semantic margins.
- `crates/yzn-config/src/main.rs:476`: custom popup rendering emits per-popup `side_margin` and `vertical_margin` fields.
- `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-zellij-popup/.beads`: child bead `yazelix-zellij-popup-default-popup-margins-id3` tracks adding plugin-level default popup margins to `yzpp`.

## Scope

In scope:
- Wait for `yazelix-zellij-popup-default-popup-margins-id3` to land and be pushed.
- Update the `yazelixZellijPopup` flake input to the child commit that supports plugin-level margin defaults.
- Represent `[popup].side_margin` and `[popup].vertical_margin` through the child-owned `yzpp` default margin surface.
- Remove or simplify repeated per-popup margin rendering in packaged managed popups and custom popup rendering when the child default covers it.
- Keep per-popup explicit margins only if the child contract requires them for compatibility or override tests.
- Preserve the user-visible Next config contract exactly: defaults remain side `1`, vertical `0`; user overrides still apply to all managed popups.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard for any changed runtime/ownership behavior.

Out of scope:
- Do not move generated popup specs, keybindings, command selection, or runtime injection to the child repo.
- Do not change `[popups.<id>]` semantics.
- Do not add per-popup margin config in Yazelix Next unless separately chosen.
- Do not broaden this into a generic popup renderer extraction.
- Do not change popup lifecycle behavior.

## Implementation Notes

1. Start by checking the child bead and child commit shape.
2. Update Next to set the plugin-level default margin in the `yzpp` config block.
3. Replace the broad string replacement in `runtime/yzn.rs` with a smaller patch of the child-owned default margin surface, or remove runtime patching entirely if the generated config is materialized through a checked config helper.
4. Keep `crates/yzn-config` as the semantic config owner; it should read/validate `[popup]` values and provide only the data needed by the runtime.
5. Adjust contract tests to prove default and overridden margins affect built-in and custom popups through the child default surface.

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 if config rendering changes.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and prove the runtime contract.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` should exit 0 if the flake input/lock changes.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should install if the package changes.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` after runtime package changes.
- `git diff --check` should exit 0.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the child default margin surface is not landed or not pushed.
- Stop if consuming child defaults requires changing Next's user-facing popup margin semantics.
- Stop if the child API still forces every popup spec to repeat margins.
- Stop if the implementation starts moving generated specs or keybindings into the child repo.

## Maintenance Notes

This is a narrow extraction of generic geometry defaults, not a reversal of `yazelix-next-move-popup-renderer-owner-v70`. Next keeps generated popup specs and semantic config; `yzpp` owns reusable popup geometry defaults.
notes: Dependency decision: no new Rust crates; consume child yzpp popup_defaults KDL surface published in yazelix-zellij-popup dfe31eb and keep parsing/patching with existing Rust string contract helpers.
close_reason: Completed: published yzpp popup_defaults child commit dfe31eb, locked yazelixZellijPopup to it, moved managed popup margins into the yzpp popup_defaults block, removed repeated built-in/custom popup margin fields, updated docs/scorecard/tests, and verified cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml; nix flake check; nix flake show --all-systems; nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs; nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>; nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next; git diff --check.

title: Adopt contract-driven check-backed development
description:
## Why

Yazelix Next is fully agent-driven, so project intent must live in durable repo state rather than chat history. The current repo already points in this direction, but the method is not yet explicit enough for future agents.

## Evidence

- `AGENTS.md:13-20`: requires contract distillation, one repo owner, focused validation, and recording rejected alternatives in Beads.
- `AGENTS.md:48-49`: says important decisions should be kept in Beads instead of chat history.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:89-94`: says the main simplicity win is one owner per contract and no duplicated compatibility surface.
- `README.md:59-73`: the repo tracks owned LOC, so process changes should stay visible and justified.

## Scope

In scope:
- Track the small implementation program for adopting contract-driven, check-backed development.
- Keep the method compatible with AI-agent-first development.

Out of scope:
- Implement feature work.
- Add broad process machinery or templates that create more overhead than clarity.

## Implementation Notes

1. Complete the child beads that document the method, expose reusable checks, and map current contracts to checks.
2. Close this epic when those child beads are done or explicitly rejected.

## Verification

- `br dep tree $EPIC` should show the child work.
- `br ready` should keep implementation beads independently actionable.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the user chooses a different development method.
- Stop if a child bead would require implementing unrelated runtime features.

## Maintenance Notes

The intended method is contract-driven and check-backed. Use TDD only where it is the cheapest way to prove a small deterministic helper or regression.
close_reason: Completed: child beads documented the workflow, added flake contract checks, and added the architecture contract matrix.

title: Replace static menu popup with curated command pane
description:
## Why

The current `Alt Shift M` menu popup is a static command/key cheat sheet. That duplicates help/docs/Keys-tab discovery and does not justify a full popup surface. Main Yazelix used the menu popup more usefully as a command pane: pick an allowed command, run it, then return or close. Yazelix Next should adopt that product role, but with a deliberately small allowlist instead of main-style broad command metadata.

## Evidence

- `flake.nix:153`: `yznMenu` is currently a shell application that prints static text.
- `flake.nix:156`: the output starts with `Yazelix Next Menu` rather than launching an interactive selector.
- `flake.nix:158`: the static menu lists commands including entries that should not be menu-palette options, such as `env`, `enter`, and `reveal`.
- `flake.nix:170`: the static menu also lists popup key hints and workspace key hints, duplicating other discovery surfaces.
- `flake.nix:186`: `yzn-menu-popup` delegates to `yzn-menu` and then waits for Enter, so the popup cannot run selected commands.
- `config.kdl:94`: `Alt Shift M` toggles the managed menu popup through the `yzpp` payload `menu`.
- `runtime/yzn.rs:63`: `yzn menu` accepts no args and execs `YZN_MENU`.
- `checks/yzn-contracts.rs:149`: the contract currently verifies static menu text.
- `checks/yzn-contracts.rs:1130`: the popup helper contract only checks that the popup delegates to `/bin/yzn-menu`.

## User-Visible Behavior

`Alt Shift M` opens a Yazelix command pane instead of a static menu. The pane presents exactly these command choices and no other actions:

- `config` / open config UI
- `doctor`
- `status`
- `screen`
- `sponsor`
- `launch`
- `help`
- `tutor`

Selecting an entry runs the corresponding `yzn` command. The command pane must not expose popup actions, raw Zellij actions, shell commands, arbitrary args, maintainer commands, or generated command discovery.

## Scope

In scope:
- Replace the static `yzn-menu` output with a small interactive command selector.
- Use only the approved allowlist: `config`, `doctor`, `status`, `screen`, `sponsor`, `launch`, `help`, and `tutor`.
- Package any selector dependency needed for the helper, likely `fzf`, or implement a tiny selector only if it stays simpler and more reliable.
- Dispatch selected commands argv-style through the packaged `yzn` front door or a narrow equivalent, not by interpolating shell strings.
- Keep `Alt Shift M` and the `yzpp` menu popup identity unchanged.
- Make `yzn menu` useful from both popup and current-terminal contexts if practical.
- In popup mode, after a command that returns, keep a bounded close/return behavior similar to main Yazelix: close or return to the menu without leaving the user at a dead static screen.
- Add focused contract checks that the menu allowlist contains exactly the approved commands and excludes `env`, `enter`, `reveal`, popup toggles, and workspace key hints.
- Add a check that selected command dispatch is argv-based, not shell-string based, if this logic moves into Rust or a testable helper.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and the LOC scorecard if files change.
- Refresh installed `yzn` after runtime flake changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not port main Yazelix full command metadata or broad palette categories.
- Do not include actions such as Toggle LazyGit, Toggle config popup, Toggle agent popup, or raw `yzpp` payloads.
- Do not include `env`, `enter`, `reveal`, custom popup definitions, shell commands, or commands requiring arbitrary arguments.
- Do not add user-configurable command pools in this bead.
- Do not add fuzzy search over host commands or Zellij commands.
- Do not change popup keybindings or popup lifecycle behavior beyond the menu helper contents.
- Do not implement `tutor` here unless it is already available by implementation time; if `tutor` is still missing, include it only if the implementation can show it as disabled/coming-soon without breaking the exact allowlist decision, or block this bead on the tutor bead before coding.

## Implementation Notes

1. Decide first whether `tutor` exists by the time this bead is implemented. If not, either depend on `yazelix-next-yzx-tutor-after-keymaps-ux3` or represent it as a disabled allowlist entry with clear copy; do not silently omit it.
2. Prefer a small local Rust helper if selector/dispatch/error handling becomes awkward in shell. A shell wrapper is acceptable only if dispatch remains obviously argv-safe.
3. If using `fzf`, package it in the menu helper runtime inputs so the menu does not depend on host PATH.
4. Keep labels human-friendly, but keep the command id separate from display text.
5. Preserve the direct `yzn menu` CLI contract: it should open the same command pane or print a clear non-interactive fallback when there is no TTY.
6. Use main Yazelix `menu_commands.rs` only as reference for UX patterns, not as code to port wholesale.

## Verification

- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and prove the menu allowlist and exclusions.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` should exit 0 if package outputs change.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should exit 0.
- `git diff --check` should exit 0.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` should be run after runtime changes.
- Manual dogfood: press `Alt Shift M`, select `status` or `doctor`, confirm output is visible, then close or return to the menu; select `config` and confirm it opens the config UI.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the implementation starts becoming a general command palette or command registry.
- Stop if supporting `launch` or `config` safely from inside the popup is unclear; ask whether those entries should be disabled or removed instead of guessing.
- Stop if `tutor` is not implemented and the exact allowlist cannot be honored cleanly.
- Stop if dispatch would require shell-string parsing.
- Stop if adding selector behavior requires weakening the managed popup or Zellij sidecar ownership model.

## Maintenance Notes

Rejected alternative: keep the menu as a static cheat sheet. It is redundant and low-value. Rejected alternative: port main Yazelix command metadata and broad palette eligibility. Next only wants the eight chosen commands for now. Rejected alternative: include popup actions. The user explicitly chose commands only, no actions.
close_reason: Completed: yzn menu now opens a curated eight-command pane, dispatches selections through the yzn front door with fixed argv, the popup inherits YZN_MENU_YZN for managed dispatch, docs/LOC were updated, contract checks cover allowlist/exclusions/dispatch, Nix checks passed, and the installed runtime was refreshed.

title: Track agent popup cwd-mismatch restart
description:
## Why

The underlying behavior belongs to `yazelix-zellij-popup`, but Yazelix Next is where the user notices it: `Alt Shift L` can show an old hidden agent pane after a tab/workspace cwd change. The maintainer does not routinely triage the popup repo, so YZN should track the cross-repo integration work that consumes the child fix once it lands.

## Evidence

- `config.kdl`: YZN defines the managed `agent` popup in the `yzpp` popup config.
- `config.kdl`: the agent uses `toggle_close_behavior "hide"`, so the popup plugin decides whether to reuse, hide, close, or restart the pane.
- `config.kdl`: `Alt Shift L` toggles the `agent` popup through `MessagePlugin "yzpp"`.
- `runtime/yzn-agent.rs`: the popup command chooses and persists the first available provider from `codex resume`, `grok`, `opencode`, `pi`, then `claude --resume`.
- Child planning bead: `yazelix-zellij-popup-9lw` in `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-zellij-popup` tracks the actual plugin behavior change: restart a hidden keep-alive popup when its cwd differs from the current focused cwd.

## Scope

In scope:
- Track the YZN integration after `yazelix-zellij-popup-9lw` lands.
- Update the `yazelixZellijPopup` flake input to the child commit that implements cwd-mismatch restart.
- Add or update YZN changelog/docs only if the user-visible runtime contract changes.
- Run the YZN runtime checks and update the installed `yazelix-next` profile.
- Manual dogfood in a fresh YZN session: launch agent in cwd A, hide/replace it, change tab/workspace cwd to B, toggle agent, and confirm the old hidden agent pane is replaced by a fresh selected-provider agent pane in cwd B.

Out of scope:
- Do not implement cwd-mismatch detection in YZN config or wrappers.
- Do not add a separate YZN restart-agent key unless the child plugin cannot support the default toggle behavior.
- Do not create multiple cwd-scoped agent panes in this bead; the chosen behavior is replacement because agents are ephemeral and resumable.
- Do not restart the maintainer's live session from automation.
- Do not change provider bootstrap or `Alt Shift L` key configurability in this bead.

## Implementation Notes

1. Start by checking `br show yazelix-zellij-popup-9lw` in the popup repo and the corresponding pushed child commit.
2. Update YZN with `nix flake update yazelixZellijPopup` after the child commit is pushed.
3. Keep YZN's `config.kdl` shape as `agent { toggle_close_behavior "hide" }` unless the child contract explicitly changes.
4. If the child plugin cannot reliably compare cwd for hidden/suppressed panes, record that result in this bead and choose whether to keep the current workaround or add an explicit restart key.

## Verification

- `nix flake check` should exit 0.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` should exit 0 if the lock update changes evaluated outputs.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` should update the installed runtime.
- Manual fresh-session dogfood should confirm the agent cwd-mismatch replacement behavior.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the child popup bead is not closed or the required child commit has not been pushed.
- Stop if YZN would need to duplicate popup lifecycle logic.
- Stop if the behavior cannot be validated without a fresh YZN session; report the manual test requirement instead of claiming completion.

## Maintenance Notes

- Cross-repo owner: `yazelix-zellij-popup` owns lifecycle logic; YZN owns consuming the packaged wasm and documenting/dogfooding the runtime contract.
- Rejected alternative: cwd-scoped identities with multiple hidden agents. More complete, but more stateful than the current low-priority need.
- Rejected alternative: explicit restart-agent key. It is a fallback if plugin cwd comparison proves impossible, not the preferred UX.

title: Decide yzn key remaps
description:
Decide the small first-class key remap set for yzn before adding more bindings. Current owned Zellij config remaps Alt n to Alt m for new stacked panes and uses Alt Shift h to show/hide the sidebar.

Acceptance criteria:
- List the remaps yzn should own, including current Alt m and Alt Shift h behavior.
- Compare against main Yazelix muscle memory, Zellij defaults, Mars/window-manager conflicts, and Yazi/Helix/Nushell keys.
- Mark each candidate as keep, add, change, or reject, with a short reason.
- Choose one owner for each accepted remap, normally config.kdl unless another layer is clearly better.
- Do not implement remaps in this bead unless the user explicitly chooses the final set.
close_reason: Decided and implemented the first yzn key policy: Ctrl Alt owns native Zellij mode control for lock/pane/tab/resize/search/session/quit, Ctrl Alt h/j/k/l owns direct tab/pane movement, Alt m remains new stacked pane, Alt Shift h remains sidebar toggle, and Move mode is intentionally unbound. Packaged config is now covered by yzn-contracts.

title: Document the contract-driven agent workflow
description:
## Why

The repo has the seeds of the workflow, but future agents need the final method written in current-state project instructions: contract first, one owner, smallest implementation, focused check, rejected alternatives in Beads. This prevents generic TDD, broad compatibility surfaces, and mechanical ports from main Yazelix.

## Evidence

- `AGENTS.md:13-20`: contract distillation exists but does not name the final method or explain when TDD is appropriate.
- `AGENTS.md:61-68`: verification guidance exists but is command-oriented, not tied to feature contracts.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:89-94`: architecture doc already frames one owner per contract as the real simplicity win.

## Scope

In scope:
- `AGENTS.md`
- `ARCHITECTURE.md`
- A short README pointer only if it improves discoverability.

Out of scope:
- Source code changes.
- New feature work.
- A heavyweight process template file.

## Implementation Notes

1. Add a concise section naming the method: contract-driven, check-backed development.
2. Define the loop: contract, owner, check, implementation, verification, rejected alternatives.
3. State when TDD is useful: Rust helpers, parsers, regressions, deterministic CLI behavior.
4. State when TDD is not the default: layout design, runtime integration, fork decisions, dogfooding surfaces.
5. Keep wording terse enough for agents to follow quickly.

## Verification

- `git diff --check` should exit 0.
- `wc -l .gitignore AGENTS.md README.md ARCHITECTURE.md flake.nix mars.toml config.kdl layout.kdl layout.swap.kdl nu/config.nu nu/env.nu helix/config.toml yazi/init.lua yazi/plugins/sidebar-status.yazi/main.lua yazi/yazi.toml crates/yzn-open/Cargo.toml crates/yzn-open/src/main.rs checks/zellij-layout.rs runtime/yzn-nu.rs` should match the README scorecard if docs change.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if documenting the method would require choosing new product scope.
- Stop if the docs become a generic process essay instead of actionable agent guidance.

## Maintenance Notes

Do not call the default process TDD. The stronger name is contract-driven, check-backed development.
close_reason: Completed: AGENTS.md names contract-driven, check-backed development; states when TDD fits and when it is not the default; verification guidance includes nix flake check. Verified git diff --check, LOC scorecard, nix flake show, nix flake check, nix build, temp profile install, and profile refresh.

title: Use Nushell as default pane shell
description: Make the packaged Yazelix Next Zellij config start panes with Nushell as the default shell, while keeping the change small and reversible.
acceptance_criteria:
config.kdl declares default_shell using a Nix-substituted Nushell path.
yzn runtime includes Nushell on PATH.
The packaged share/yazelix-next/config.kdl contains an absolute /nix/store nu path.
nix flake show, nix build .#yzn, temp profile install, and profile refresh pass.
README LOC scorecard is updated.
close_reason: Completed: user dogfooding confirms Nushell default shell is acceptable; flake show, build, temp profile install, live profile refresh, and config/wrapper inspection passed.

title: Expose flake checks for runtime contracts
description:
## Why

Agent-driven development needs one deterministic command that proves the core runtime contracts without launching a GUI. The repo currently has packages/apps and build-time guards, but no `checks` output that an agent can run as the default contract suite.

## Evidence

- `AGENTS.md:61-68`: runtime changes normally require `nix flake show --all-systems`, `nix build .#yzn`, and a profile install check.
- `flake.nix:39-44`: local Rust helpers are compiled inside Nix.
- `flake.nix:145-149`: `yzn-layout-check` validates the Zellij layout during the package build.
- `runtime/yzn-nu.rs:24-49`: `yzn-nu` owns generated Nushell config layering and exec behavior, but its smoke test is manual.

## Scope

In scope:
- `flake.nix`
- `checks/` or small Nix check derivations
- Existing Rust helpers if exposing checks requires tiny adjustments.

Out of scope:
- GUI launch tests.
- Home Manager or desktop environment test infrastructure.
- Broad CI setup unless the user separately chooses it.

## Implementation Notes

1. Add `checks.${system}` outputs for cheap runtime contracts.
2. Include at least: package build, Zellij layout template/swap validation, and `yzn-nu` config layering with a temporary config root.
3. Keep checks non-interactive and independent from the user's real home/config.
4. Prefer Nix derivations that execute the exact built artifacts over separate mock implementations.
5. Document the chosen command in `AGENTS.md` or README if it becomes the recommended default.

## Verification

- `nix flake check` should exit 0 on the current system.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should still exit 0.
- `git diff --check` should exit 0.
- README LOC scorecard should be updated if owned files change.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if a check requires opening Mars or a graphical Zellij session.
- Stop if the check duplicates large runtime behavior instead of executing the built helper.
- Stop if `nix flake check` becomes so heavy that agents avoid running it.

## Maintenance Notes

This bead is about agent affordance: one obvious command should prove the contracts that currently require remembering ad hoc commands from chat.
close_reason: Completed: flake.nix exposes checks for yzn and contracts. nix flake check builds yzn and runs a non-GUI yzn-nu temporary-config smoke test that proves packaged config, user layering, carapace, and zoxide. Verified nix flake check and nix build .#yzn.

title: Fix yzn reveal in managed sessions
description:
## Why

`yzn reveal` is advertised and wired, but the user reports it still does not work in real Yazelix Next sessions. This breaks both the explicit CLI surface (`yzn reveal <target>`) and the managed Helix `Alt r` reveal binding. The implementation already has multiple checked pieces, so the next pass should reproduce the live failure and fix the broken runtime boundary instead of adding more static wiring checks.

## Evidence

- User report on 2026-07-02: `yzn reveal` is still not working in the dogfooded Yazelix Next session.
- `runtime/yzn.rs:85`: the `reveal` command dispatches to `exec_reveal`.
- `runtime/yzn.rs:118`: `exec_reveal` runs the packaged `yzn-reveal` helper and injects `YZN_YA` plus `YZN_ZELLIJ`.
- `crates/yzn-open/src/bin/yzn-reveal.rs:36`: the helper resolves the target to an existing absolute path before reveal.
- `crates/yzn-open/src/bin/yzn-reveal.rs:37`: the helper asks the pane orchestrator for `get_active_tab_session_state`.
- `crates/yzn-open/src/bin/yzn-reveal.rs:38`: the helper requires `/sidebar_yazi/yazi_id`; if registration is absent, reveal fails before reaching Yazi.
- `crates/yzn-open/src/bin/yzn-reveal.rs:39`: the helper calls `ya emit-to <yazi_id> reveal <target>`.
- `crates/yzn-open/src/bin/yzn-reveal.rs:48`: after the Yazi reveal command, the helper asks the pane orchestrator to focus the sidebar.
- `yazi/plugins/sidebar-state.yazi/main.lua:34`: the Yazi sidebar plugin shells out to `zellij action pipe` to register sidebar state.
- `yazi/plugins/sidebar-state.yazi/main.lua:60`: registration depends on `YAZI_ID`, `ZELLIJ_PANE_ID`, and Yazi cwd all being available.
- `runtime/yzn-yazi.rs:60`: the Yazi launcher saves `ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME` into `YAZELIX_ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME` and clears `ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME` before execing Yazi.
- `../yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator/src/sidebar_yazi.rs:36`: the pane orchestrator accepts `register_sidebar_yazi_state` only after permissions are granted and only when the pane id matches the managed sidebar pane.
- `../yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator/src/sidebar_yazi.rs:63`: registration returns missing when the submitted pane id cannot be mapped to the active tab sidebar.
- `helix/config.toml:9`: managed Helix binds `A-r` to `:sh yzn reveal "%{buffer_name}"`.
- `checks/yzn-contracts.rs:860`: current checks only prove packaged fragments exist; they do not prove live registration, `ya emit-to`, or sidebar focus works in a real session.

## User-Visible Behavior

Inside a managed Yazelix Next session:

- Running `yzn reveal <existing-file-or-directory>` reveals that target in the active tab's managed Yazi sidebar.
- The Yazi sidebar becomes visible/focused after reveal.
- Pressing managed Helix `Alt r` reveals the current buffer path in the same active tab's Yazi sidebar.
- Failure cases print a clear bounded error naming the broken boundary: missing sidebar registration, failed `ya emit-to`, failed pane-orchestrator focus, missing target, or wrong session.

## Scope

In scope:
- Reproduce the current failure in a fresh managed session before changing code.
- Trace the live pipeline end-to-end: sidebar-state Yazi plugin registration, pane-orchestrator active-tab state, `yzn-reveal` session env, `ya emit-to ... reveal`, and sidebar focus.
- Fix the smallest broken boundary in Yazelix Next or the pane-orchestrator child repo, depending on where the failure is found.
- If the child pane-orchestrator needs a change, create/update the child repo bead and then consume the fixed flake input here.
- Add a focused non-GUI check where possible for the discovered regression class. Examples: environment propagation, pane-id normalization, missing registration diagnostics, or helper command construction.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and the LOC scorecard if user-visible behavior, checks, or file ownership changes.
- Refresh installed `yzn` after runtime flake changes.

Out of scope:
- Do not add generic workspace command control.
- Do not add arbitrary reveal targets beyond one existing path argument.
- Do not implement first-class Neovim reveal integration here; host-editor reveal belongs to `yazelix-next-add-host-owned-editor-command-support-5pf` or a later editor-specific bead.
- Do not replace Yazi or fork Yazi.
- Do not weaken pane-orchestrator ownership of sidebar focus/pane state.
- Do not add broad GUI automation unless a focused manual dogfood step is the only way to verify the final behavior.

## Implementation Notes

1. Start with manual/diagnostic reproduction in a fresh session: inspect what `yzn reveal <file>` prints, then check whether `get_active_tab_session_state` contains `sidebar_yazi`.
2. If `sidebar_yazi` is null, inspect the Yazi plugin registration path: `YAZI_ID`, `ZELLIJ_PANE_ID`, `YAZELIX_ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME`, `YZN_ZELLIJ`, and pane id normalization against the orchestrator's managed sidebar id.
3. If registration exists, inspect `ya emit-to <id> reveal <path>` semantics and whether the event name/argument shape matches current Yazi.
4. If Yazi reveals but focus fails, inspect the pane-orchestrator `focus_sidebar` response and active-tab targeting.
5. Preserve the direct `yzn reveal <target>` CLI; do not make reveal depend only on Helix bindings.
6. Improve diagnostics only where they help identify the failing boundary; avoid noisy logging in normal successful reveal.

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-open/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 and cover any changed helper parsing/command/session behavior.
- If pane-orchestrator changes are needed, run that repo's focused tests for sidebar Yazi registration/session state/focus and record the exact commands in this bead when implemented.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and include the packaged runtime contract checks.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` should exit 0 if package outputs or flake inputs change.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should exit 0.
- `git diff --check` should exit 0.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` should be run after runtime changes.
- Manual dogfood: in a fresh managed session, create or choose an existing file, run `yzn reveal <path>`, verify Yazi selects/reveals it and the sidebar is focused; then open that file in managed Helix and verify `Alt r` does the same.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the failure cannot be reproduced in a fresh managed session; record the exact tested revision, command, and observed behavior before closing or revising the bead.
- Stop if the fix belongs entirely in `yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator`; create/update the child bead and make this bead consume that fix rather than duplicating logic in YZN.
- Stop if current Yazi no longer supports the assumed `ya emit-to <id> reveal <target>` contract; record the new required Yazi command shape before changing wrappers.
- Stop if the implementation starts broadening reveal into editor-specific or voice/AI command-control work.

## Maintenance Notes

Current static checks are not enough: they prove the helper and config fragments are packaged, but not that the live Yazi/sidebar/orchestrator registration loop works. The bug should be closed only after a real managed-session reveal path is verified.
close_reason: Completed

title: Make managed popups flush to the pane area
description:
## Why

The current 95% popup geometry is usable and preserves the Yazelix top bar and Zellij bottom status bar, but on large panes it leaves visible side and bottom gutters that make the surface feel slightly accidental. The chosen UX direction is that every managed popup should fill the available pane area while still leaving the persistent bars visible. Agent, LazyGit, config, and menu popups should feel like focused workspace overlays rather than small modals.

## Evidence

- `config.kdl:7`: the packaged `config` popup is declared in the `yzpp` popup block.
- `config.kdl:10`: the packaged config popup uses `width_percent 95`.
- `config.kdl:11`: the packaged config popup uses `height_percent 95`.
- `config.kdl:14`: the packaged `agent` popup is declared in the same managed popup block.
- `config.kdl:17`: the packaged agent popup uses `width_percent 95`.
- `config.kdl:18`: the packaged agent popup uses `height_percent 95`.
- `config.toml:22`: root semantic config exposes `[popup]`.
- `config.toml:24`: the current default popup size is `95`.
- `runtime/yzn.rs:552`: runtime materialization patches packaged popup geometry when `popup.size` differs from the default.
- `checks/yzn-contracts.rs:1037`: packaged popup contract checks currently expect `width_percent 95` and `height_percent 95` for all managed popups.
- `ARCHITECTURE.md:249`: the runtime contract says `popup.size` controls generated popup width and height, while visual popup behavior remains manual dogfooding.

## Scope

In scope:
- Make the default managed popup geometry flush to the pane area for all packaged popups: config, agent, LazyGit, and menu.
- Prefer the smallest implementation: likely change the default `popup.size` from `95` to `100` and update packaged `config.kdl` popup geometry to `width_percent 100` and `height_percent 100`.
- Preserve the persistent Yazelix top bar and Zellij bottom status bar; flush means filling the pane area, not covering global workspace chrome.
- Keep existing user-configurable `popup.size` unless implementation proves it undermines the chosen default or creates confusing UI. If kept, document that values below 100 intentionally create an inset popup.
- Update checks that assert packaged popup geometry and custom popup-size replacement.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and the LOC scorecard when files change.
- Refresh installed `yzn` after runtime flake changes.
- Manual dogfood with at least the agent popup and one short-lived popup to confirm the new geometry visually aligns with the pane area.

Out of scope:
- Do not redesign popup lifecycle, focus, stacking, or keep-alive behavior.
- Do not add per-popup geometry in this bead.
- Do not implement custom popup definitions; that belongs to `yazelix-next-6aq`.
- Do not change top-bar, bottom-bar, or tab-bar layout.
- Do not make popups cover the global bars.
- Do not add a new crate or helper for geometry.
- Do not change popup keybindings.

## Implementation Notes

1. Start by stating the contract in the implementation/check: managed popups fill the pane area by default and bars remain visible.
2. Change the shared default first, then update packaged `config.kdl` literals and runtime replacement markers together so custom popup sizes still materialize correctly.
3. Update `crates/yzn-config` catalog/default tests so Ratconfig shows the new default consistently.
4. Update `checks/yzn-contracts.rs` expected packaged geometry counts from 95 to 100 and keep a custom-size test to prove user overrides still replace all managed popup blocks.
5. If `width_percent 100` or `height_percent 100` in `yzpp` covers the bars or renders incorrectly, stop and create/update a child `yazelix-zellij-popup` bead instead of hacking around it in YZN.

## Verification

- `cargo test --manifest-path crates/yzn-config/Cargo.toml` should exit 0 and prove the root config/Ratconfig default is `popup.size = 100`.
- `nix flake check` should exit 0 and prove packaged popup geometry and custom popup-size replacement contracts still hold.
- `nix flake show --all-systems` should exit 0 if package outputs change.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` should exit 0.
- `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>` should exit 0.
- `git diff --check` should exit 0.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` should be run after runtime changes.
- Manual dogfood: open `Alt Shift L` agent popup, `Alt Shift J` LazyGit or `Alt Shift K` config popup, and verify the popup fills the pane area while the Yazelix top bar and Zellij bottom status bar remain visible.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if `yzpp` interprets 100% as covering or colliding with global bars.
- Stop if the implementation needs a child popup plugin geometry change; record that dependency instead of adding YZN-side hacks.
- Stop if preserving user `popup.size` becomes incompatible with the default flush decision; ask whether to remove or demote the setting before coding.
- Stop if visual dogfooding shows that the menu/config popup needs a different design decision from the agent/LazyGit popup.

## Maintenance Notes

Rejected alternative: keep 95% as a generic modal-style default. The product decision is to favor usable surface area and clean pane-area alignment for all managed popups. Rejected alternative: make only the agent popup flush. The user chose every popup flush for consistency.
close_reason: Completed: popup.size now defaults to 100, packaged popup geometry uses 100% width and height, contracts/docs updated, runtime upgraded, and manual dogfood confirmed the popups are flush without covering the bars.

title: Add semantic config for managed shell integrations
description:
## Why

Yazelix Next currently initializes Carapace, Zoxide, and Starship inside the managed Nushell runtime by default. That is the right default, but users may eventually need a small supported way to disable or inspect those managed shell integrations without replacing the whole Nushell config or editing packaged files.

## Current Contract

Inside `yzn`-managed Nushell:

- Carapace completions are generated and sourced by default.
- Zoxide init is generated and sourced by default.
- Starship owns the left and right prompt by default.
- Optional user files under `~/.config/yazelix-next/nu/env.nu` and `config.nu` are layered after packaged config.
- Normal user Nushell config is not loaded by default.

## User-Visible Behavior

A user can use the Yazelix semantic config surface, and later Ratconfig, to inspect and optionally toggle managed shell integrations such as Carapace, Zoxide, and Starship. Defaults preserve the current curated shell behavior.

Possible first settings:

```toml
[shell]
carapace = true
zoxide = true
starship = true
```

Names are illustrative; choose final names only when implementing the contract.

## Scope

In scope:
- Add the smallest semantic config surface for managed Nushell integrations if/when a real need appears.
- Keep defaults equal to current behavior: Carapace, Zoxide, and Starship enabled.
- Route all validation through the shared Yazelix config core.
- Make `runtime/yzn-nu.rs` or its owner consume the settings without loading ambient user shell config.
- Surface the settings in Ratconfig only if the config core owns them.
- Add focused checks for defaults, toggling each integration, and generated Nushell config contents.
- Update README, ARCHITECTURE, CHANGELOG, and LOC scorecard if implemented.

Out of scope:
- Do not add Bash, Zsh, Fish, or arbitrary managed-shell selection in this bead.
- Do not import the user's normal Nushell config.
- Do not build a generic shell plugin manager.
- Do not translate native shell config through Ratconfig.
- Do not add this setting before someone actually needs to disable or inspect an integration.
- Do not weaken the current managed shell reproducibility contract.

## Implementation Notes

1. Prefer booleans over a generic plugin list.
2. Keep generated Nushell config deterministic and easy to test.
3. Preserve `PATH` setup so enabled tools remain available inside managed Nushell.
4. If disabling Starship, define the resulting prompt behavior explicitly before coding.
5. Carapace support remains a curated managed-shell default, not a promise to support every external shell.

## Verification

- Config contract check proves missing settings default to current enabled behavior.
- Check proves disabling Carapace removes the generated Carapace source line and leaves Nushell launch working.
- Check proves disabling Zoxide removes the generated Zoxide source line and leaves Nushell launch working.
- Check proves disabling Starship has a clearly defined prompt result.
- Existing `yzn-nu` checks still prove enabled tools work when defaults are used.
- `nix flake check` if runtime generation changes.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs` if packaged runtime changes.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if this turns into generic shell support.
- Stop if toggles require importing ambient user shell config.
- Stop if the first requested setting is only theoretical and adds config surface without a current user workflow.
notes: Prime-beads review 2026-06-27: defer until there is a concrete user need to disable or inspect managed shell integrations. The bead remains the prerequisite for managed multi-shell selection, but should not appear as ready feature work while the toggle need is theoretical.

title: Investigate Ghostty consuming yazelix-cursors without packaging Ghostty
description:
## Why

Some users may run `yzn enter` from their own Ghostty instead of launching Yazelix through Mars. It would be valuable for those users to get Yazelix cursor effects through `yazelix-cursors` without Yazelix Next packaging or owning Ghostty itself.

## Question

Can Ghostty consume Yazelix cursor shaders/config from `yazelix-cursors` in a low-friction way that fits the current boundary?

Desired boundary:

- `yazelix-cursors` owns cursor registry, validation, Ghostty shader/include generation, and terminal target contracts.
- Yazelix Next does not package Ghostty as a supported terminal path.
- Users can configure their own Ghostty to run `yzn enter` and include/generated Yazelix cursor config.
- Yazelix Next can document or lightly wrap this path without mutating arbitrary Ghostty configs by default.

## Research Scope

In scope:
- Inspect the current `yazelix-cursors` Ghostty target and standalone `yzc` flow.
- Determine the smallest user workflow for Ghostty users:
  - install/run `yzc`
  - `yzc init`
  - `yzc generate ghostty`
  - add `config-file = ~/.config/yazelix_cursors/ghostty.conf` to Ghostty
  - configure Ghostty command to run `yzn enter`
- Check whether `yzc generate ghostty` can be made idempotent and ergonomic enough for Yazelix Next users without a separate Ghostty package.
- Evaluate whether Yazelix Next should expose a thin `yzn cursors`/`yzn setup ghostty-cursors` helper or only document the standalone `yzc` path.
- Identify what, if anything, should be changed in `yazelix-cursors` rather than this repo.
- Record concrete rejected alternatives in Beads if the decision matters later.

Out of scope:
- Do not package Ghostty in Yazelix Next.
- Do not add a Ghostty launcher or terminal matrix support.
- Do not mutate a user's Ghostty config automatically without an explicit user command and backup/preview policy.
- Do not duplicate cursor registry or shader logic in Yazelix Next.
- Do not make Mars cursor behavior depend on Ghostty config files.
- Do not add Home Manager Ghostty module support in this investigation.

## Possible Outcomes

1. Docs-only: document the standalone `yzc` flow and Ghostty `config-file` include.
2. Child-repo improvement: add or improve a `yzc setup ghostty` command in `yazelix-cursors` with preview/backup semantics.
3. Thin Yazelix delegate: add `yzn cursors` or `yzn setup ghostty-cursors` that invokes packaged `yzc`, if it avoids making users learn another command without taking over Ghostty.
4. Defer: conclude the standalone flow is not worth integrating until after public swap or macOS/Mars validation.

## Verification For A Future Implementation

- `yzc generate ghostty` produces a Ghostty include and shader files deterministically.
- The generated include is usable from a normal Ghostty config through `config-file = ...`.
- The documented Ghostty command can run `yzn enter` without Yazelix packaging Ghostty.
- Any helper command is explicit, idempotent, and does not overwrite user Ghostty config without confirmation/backup.
- Docs clearly distinguish Mars-backed `yzn launch` from user-terminal `yzn enter`.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if the solution requires packaging Ghostty.
- Stop if the solution requires broad terminal support matrix work.
- Stop if safe Ghostty config mutation becomes the main task; defer to `yazelix-cursors` or docs-only.
- Stop if the user workflow is already good enough with standalone `yzc` and a short doc section.

title: Start Herdr-inspired yzn session attach flow
description:
## Why

The Herdr research bead proved the useful direction but intentionally stopped before implementation. The first practical Yazelix Next step is not to build a multiplexer or adopt Mars Next live-session ownership. It is to make `yzn` consume Zellij's existing session primitives so closing a window or starting another `yzn` can attach to the right runtime state without breaking the Yazi-to-Helix bridge.

## User-Visible Behavior To Distill

A user should have an obvious way to reopen the same Yazelix Next session after detach/close, while existing panes keep their identity and file opens from Yazi continue targeting the correct Helix bridge. Creating a fresh independent session should remain possible, but it must be explicit enough that bridge/session state does not leak between windows.

## Source Context

- Herdr research: `yazelix-next-t1w`.
- Herdr model: background server owns live PTYs; clients attach/detach; session JSON stores shape, not arbitrary process handles; live handoff is deferred as too expensive.
- Current Yazelix Next launch: `flake.nix` launches Mars then Zellij with `--new-session-with-layout`, which biases every `yzn` start toward a fresh generated Zellij session.
- Current bridge isolation: `YAZELIX_HELIX_BRIDGE_SESSION_ID` is top-level-launch-scoped, and `yzn-open` also checks `ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME` when available.
- Zellij primitives: named sessions, `attach --create`, list sessions, kill/delete sessions, background creation, `on_force_close detach`, and session serialization.
- Mars Next context: `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/mars-next` has a single-terminal `mars-next-live-session` PTY boundary, but it has no multiplexer server/client model, no detach/attach, no workspace/tab model, and no socket API. It is future terminal-core evidence, not the current yzn session owner.

## Scope

In scope:
- Define the first `yzn` session contract before coding: what plain `yzn` means, how a fresh session is requested, and which command surface is intentionally absent.
- Prefer a tiny surface around Zellij sessions, likely stable default attach/create plus one explicit fresh-session path if needed.
- Preserve managed Mars launch, packaged Zellij config, packaged layout, Yazi sidebar, Helix bridge, and current helper binaries.
- Make bridge identity session-scoped enough that attaching to an existing Zellij session does not mint a conflicting `YAZELIX_HELIX_BRIDGE_SESSION_ID` for already-running panes.
- Apply the managed layout only when creating or resurrecting a session, not blindly on every attach.
- Add the cheapest check that proves command construction and bridge/session identity behavior without launching a GUI.
- Update `README.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and the LOC scorecard if runtime behavior changes.
- Refresh the installed `yzn` runtime after changing the flake runtime.

Out of scope:
- Do not build a background Yazelix daemon.
- Do not own PTYs, transfer file descriptors, or implement Herdr live handoff.
- Do not consume `mars-next-live-session` for this slice.
- Do not add agent sidebar/state detection yet.
- Do not add broad CLI session management if a smaller attach/create contract is enough.
- Do not expose Zellij-native config sidecar or bar work in this bead.

## Implementation Notes

1. First inspect the packaged Yazelix Zellij CLI behavior for `attach --create`, `--layout`, `--new-session-with-layout`, `--session`, `session_name`, and `attach_to_session` options without launching a GUI.
2. Decide whether the smallest safe launch shape is CLI-driven (`zellij attach --create <name> --layout <layout> ...`) or config/layout-driven (`session_name`, `attach_to_session`, `default_layout`).
3. Keep the session name stable and Yazelix-owned. A likely first default is a single named session such as `yzn`, but this must be checked against existing generated-name/title behavior and multi-window expectations.
4. If supporting fresh independent sessions, prefer an explicit command or flag rather than changing plain `yzn` ambiguity.
5. Derive `YAZELIX_HELIX_BRIDGE_SESSION_ID` from the durable Zellij session name or another stable session-scoped value for attached sessions. Do not use a new random top-level client id when attaching to existing panes.
6. Extend Rust contract checks if command assembly moves into Rust. If the launcher stays shell/Nix, add a focused check that validates the packaged launcher text/argv and bridge id rules without running Mars or Zellij interactively.
7. Manual dogfooding is required before claiming UX correctness, but automated checks should catch the identity/layout regression class first.

## Verification

- Focused contract check for the new launch/session command shape.
- Focused bridge/session identity check proving attach does not create a conflicting helper id.
- `nix flake check`.
- `nix flake show --all-systems`.
- `nix build .#yzn --no-link --print-build-logs`.
- Temp profile install with `nix profile add --refresh /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-dir/yazelix-next --profile <tmp>`.
- `nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix-next` after runtime changes.
- Manual dogfooding: create session, detach/close, reopen/attach, open from Yazi into the existing Helix bridge, then create a fresh independent session if that surface is implemented.

## Stop Conditions

- Stop if Zellij cannot attach/create with the managed layout without creating duplicate startup panes.
- Stop if bridge identity cannot be made session-scoped without breaking standalone helper behavior.
- Stop if the smallest implementation starts becoming a session manager framework.
- Stop if user-visible semantics for plain `yzn` versus fresh session are unclear; ask for the decision before coding.

## Rejected For This Slice

- Herdr-style PTY master ownership and SCM_RIGHTS handoff.
- A Yazelix background daemon or socket API.
- Mars Next live-session as the yzn session owner.
- Agent-state/sidebar work before session attach/detach is correct.
